title: No Star Quality
body: Avoid buying this product! Just fight the urge and temptation. I am not happy with this device at all. The short version is this device does not actually work. The not short but not long version is I had one, that one didn’t work, they sent another one, don’t know if that one works or not because it came with no accessories or power cords, and the third one started messing up within the first week. I’m limited to what I can do about it because it was a Christmas gift but I really feel bad that the person was thoughtful enough to get this for me but I am not enjoying it at all.The full story is, when I open the first one, it’s seem to be fine but after a day or two, the device would not turn on. I saw online that if you unplug the cord from the device and wait about 30 seconds or so it will start to work again once plugged back in. Mind you most HDMI ports are on the back of the television so not something you want to do often. So, it worked for a couple of days (not using it everyday however) but again, I went to turn it on and....nothing. So, I have to go behind the tv again and do the whole unplug/plug in thing again. Probably did this about two more times within the next couple of weeks. At this point, it still hasn’t been a month. One morning I was using it before work but then it froze and stopped working. I went to work and when I came home the device was off. I then turned it back on and it seem to be working fine. I watched a couple of videos and at some point I paused it to go do something in another room. When I came back it was turned off. I turned it on and it seem to still be fine but when I went to play something, the screen was green and I could only hear the audio. This happened on prime and Netflix. At this point I called and reported it in which the operator said just unplug it and then plug it back in even though I told her I been doing that. She strongly insisted i do that and was trying to avoid sending me a replacement. So, again I do the unplugging thing again and of course it works but I explain to the operator that I do not want to have to go behind my tv and unplug stuff every time I want to watch something. I shouldn’t have to. That’s when I was told to send the device to be inspected. If something was wrong, I would be sent a new one, and if it was something fixable, they will fix it and send it back to me. Not even three days after I mailed it it out, I get a message saying I should expect a new one in about three days. So, there was something wrong with the first one. So, the second one comes in the mail and I knew something was off right away. They sent me just the device and nothing else. A refurbished one at that. The problem is, the packing slip says send everything back which I did but later found out in a separate email they sent, it stated do not send the plug or remote. This is something they should be more clear about and not have two different instructions but I do take partial blame for that. The other issue is, I was told I would get a new one so why send a refurbished one that was probably older than the one I sent in that I only used for one month. I called in with my concerns and the operator told me to just order a new one and they will refund me the money once I let them know I received it. I thought that was the craziest thing ever and I went back and forth with the operator about why I cannot and will not do that. I eventually asked for a supervisor which he avoided transferring me to and kept telling me they will just tell me the same thing. I asked a second time and then he finally transferred me over to his supervisor. This conversation was much shorter than the previous one and the supervisor had me put a new one in my cart, applied a credit to my account for the total of it, and then had me place the order all while on the phone with her. This was something I asked the previous person if was possible and they kept telling me no. This device came with a different remote so it was like an upgraded version. I get the new one and it seems to be okay the first day. The second day is when I have a problem turning it on. Again, I research to see if others are having the same problem. I saw with this updated one it says unplug it from the wall (the previous one said unplug from device) and it will fix the problem. Let me tell you, that’s just a temporary fix. Within one week, I had to unplug it and plug it back in three times. Right away, I tried to call it in. Could not get a response to the call. A recording would answer asking what department I needed, I said fire stick, call went blank. I tried the call back function, my phone would ring and I would answer and then it would disconnect. Tried this multiple times and it kept happening. Reached out to amazon on twitter and they had no idea there was an issue. This was just about one week ago. Since then, I haven’t spoken to anyone, it froze one time, and I have had to unplug it 3 or 4 more times, once being tonight and once last night. Nobody should have to go through these hoops every time they want to use this device so I figured it was best to write a warning so no one else has to go through this (I hardly ever write reviews). I hate that this is the experience that I have had but I hope no one else does.

title: BEAWARE SURROUND SOUND USERS
body: The installation of the "Firetv stick" has been a NIGHTMARE. I have a surround sound system consisting of a large screen SAMSUNG HDTV, Yamaha receiver, Sony DVD, and a Time Warner cable box. The system was professionally set up several years ago, and was turnkey operation for me. Up until recently I have been able to stream movies from Amazon prime through Sony DVD. It was accessing my Sony DVD that I learned Amazon had made a change and that I now required to obtain the "Firetv stick". The Firetv stick when ordered appeared to be on backlog, and after almost three weeks, I was asked to reorder or get my money back, so I decided to reorder. What I thought would take me a half hour to install, ended up three (3) hours later frustrated and with the need to contact Time Warner to get my cable reset probably. The initial first two steps went well with the Firetv stick, then everything went down hill from there. After the registration of the device I was asked to select if the sound should come out of the TV or the receiver. I selected the Yamaha receiver, however when I tested the output of the sound it still seen to come from the TV, and not the five (5) surround sound speakers which concerned me. The next prompt appeared to asked what TV or music app's I wanted. This is were it really got BAD!. I selected the apps with the remote, but then I could not get out of that step. I looked for customer service phone number to call to get help, but none was listed on the instructions or box. That led me to log on to my lap top and get on the internet for Amazon to locate a Customer service number. In the meantime my beautiful surround sound system was a mess, and I could NOT even access my cable TV. I was stuck with the app screen showing on my TV. When I contacted Amazon customer service the initial person was not knowledgeable about the Firetv stick and had to transfer me to someone else, who again was not knowledgeable but leading to a third transfer. All of this probably took half hour by itself. Once I was connected to someone who was knowledgeable, after what appeared to be almost hour on line with him indicated that the connection was established were I could stream movies. However, now I was having all kind of problems with my cable tv, which during that point, he could do no further help, and I was left needing to call my Cable provider see if they could correct the mess and at the minimum get me back to the operation I had before going down the road to install the Firetv stick. Right now I have not tried streaming a movie, and I do not know if the "Alexa" feature is engaged. It would have helped if there was a customer service number in the instructions to someone knowledgeable about the installation to call for help and assistance. My system is set up where I press one button to cut on the whole surround sound system. I since believe in my case you cannot do that to install the Firetv stick. Cable has to be disengaged or not cut on. I will attempt this weekend to see if I can stream a movie. Right now I am too frustrated and afraid of messing up my surround sound system, which is to bad. Before this I was able to stream movies using my Sony DVD with beautiful and magnificent surround sound effects. No more need to go to the movies. So, yes this has left a very bad taste on I guess what is suppose to be a better streaming and entertaining experience using the Firetv stickFRUSTUSTED!!FOLLOW-UP: I decided to go back to the audio/video shop who sold and installed my surround sound system. They explained the FireTV Stick would NOT enable the surround sound. They recommended Roku which provides streaming, access to Amazon Prime, and can be connected directly to the Yamaha Receiver instead of the TV, and it WORKED! Thru Roku the connection with the receiver, I now again I'm able to stream movies through Amazon Prime, and have beautiful surround sound.

title: BAD REMOTE BATTERY COMPARTMENT DESIGN; NO VUDU MOVIE APP
body: [[VIDEOID:e8880f600f48873ca819a8b93e7234ba]] Update 9-17-19: The sound is terrible, using the Fire Stick, compared to using my Sony Blue-ray player, which Amazon is dropping for Prime Video this month. I have the Fire Stick plugged into an HDMI port on my Denon AV receiver as is my Sony Blue-ray player. The sound using the Fire Stick is muffled and very low quality. I am very disappointed with the Fire Stick. If you have never watched Prime videos using a Blue-ray player and AVR for multi-channel sound or just have it plugged into your TV you probably think the sound is all right. For me it is a huge step backwards and I will be exploring other options to get Prime Video, i.e. new Blue-ray or 4K player that supports Prime. It's not worth returning as I used an Amazon discount coupon, provide by Amazon, towards its purchase. Also noticed the prices dropped on both the standard and 4K Fire Sticks the week after I purchased mine. The 4K version is now less than the standard version I bought, not sure if the 4K version would have better sound or not. Too late to find out now. I think there are better options out there anyway for watching Prime Video.I purchased the Fire Stick because Amazon notified me that my Sony Blue-ray player won’t be supported for using Amazon Prime video after September of this year. I was given a coupon code for a discount on the purchase of a Fire TV product and I chose to use it on this.I almost sent this back because I, like others have mentioned, couldn't get the battery door open to install the batteries into the remote. The instructions on the sleeve the remote came in were useless, for me anyway, in getting the door off to install the batteries into the remote. I tried following those instructions pushing with my left thumb on the depression on the back of the remote while holding the remote in my right hand and pushing down. The door would not budge and I was worried about mashing the remote buttons while trying to apply enough force to open the door. I requested a customer support call and the first person I talked to was no help as he just tried to walk me through the instructions I had already tried. I wanted to talk to someone that could have a new remote, in hand, to open and let me know if the instructions worked for them or if they knew some trick to opening it. My call was cut off when I was transferred so I tried to open it, some more to no avail, before requesting another call back. The next person I talked to could offer no insight as to why the door wouldn’t open and said he had two at home and they both opened with no problems. We were discussing various options i.e. return, etc., and I kept trying to open it while we talked and finally got it to open.The trick is to forget about using the instructions and using two hands and the depression seen in the first photo with your thumb. All you have to do is hold the remote, as in picture two and apply light pressure while pushing down and the remote will slide open easily.As for the Fire TV stick itself I haven’t had it long enough yet to give it a fair evaluation. A big negative, I noticed right away, though, is no Vudu movie app. I have quite a few movies in my Vudu library and my Sony Blue Ray player supported Vudu. I did find a hack, online, to add Vudu to the Fire Stick though. So far, I would say, I am less than impressed with the Fire Stick compared to my Sony Blue-ray player.

title: DONT BOTHER. BUY CHROMECAST, CHEAPER AND CAST WHATEVER APP IS ON YOUR Phone.
body: I wanted to purchase the fire stick with the headphone jack on the side of the remote. What I received was the model with out it. That is NOT why I give this product such a terrible review. Let's go down the list.1) As stated, I was intending on purchasing the model with a 3.5 mm head phone jack on the side of the remote, because my SO doesn't like to hear the shows when she's sleeping. I did not receive product that I intended to purchase. And amid covid crisis I can't return this product for fear of spreading the virus, and the idea of it being refunded and buying another one terrifies and annoys me and I refuse to further risk myself or my family catching the virus from a new fire stick I am now stuck with this.2) The second reason I wanted to buy a fire stick ( first fire stick purchase, usually I am a Chromecast kind of guy) was to help control my tv and my Alexa powered lights and Xbox. This device was supposed to act like Alexa with the Alexa hub, which I have and have installed. This feature has failed over and over and over. I have moved my Alexa hub back into the living room, because this feature almost never works. It will either not hear my command, or it acts like I didn't make one.3). The lack of apps, as I stated I love Chromecast, but chrome cast doesn't have a remote with a 3.5 mm headphone jack ( which I never received though I specifically ordered one) and it can't control my Alexa powered lights, Xbox, home, ECT. But one thing I love about Chromecast, that Roku and Firestick lack; the option to CAST apps from the the users phone. Rather than relying on apps that the device has already on its server which is what Roku and Amazon Fire Stick have. So being that I like other streaming services that I pay for monthly, ( IE Funimation.) I can't get through the app on the stick, so I can't watch it through the tv even though I pay monthly for it.4). The streaming is terrible. I have the highest wireless internet offered in my area, this device not only cuts out every few mins, but when it does "Play" it does so at such a poor frame rate that sound comes BEFORE the picture. The constant break down and buffering has me only watching from this device in a few instances.Now, normally I would never put this much effort into writing reviews, but this particular thing is something I'm passionate about.. I love being able to cast whatever I want to watch on various TV's and projectors..And this purchase from AMAZON, I honestly felt so ripped off. I WAITED WEEKS for this device, got the wrong one, can't return it due to fear of the virus, it doesn't work for a tenth of the way it's supposed to nor even in the basic way I'd like it to. And on top of all that, as if that wasn't enough, it constantly crashes out or just doesn't work.Honestly so disappointed in this device, I will be recommending people buy Chromecast from this point forward. I can't in good conscience buy another one of these products knowing I spent 39.99 for such a terrible product, and it's not even the product I ordered. You can buy a Chromecast from Walmart for $35 and tax, and you won't have to wait three weeks to get it. And you get the ability to cast ANYTHING, you want from your phone, laptop, computer, Xbox.

title: Not what I was looking for
body: I had several big problems with this. I bought it for my mother, who is technologically challenged at best, and it just wasn’t a good fit for her and I didn’t like it to boot.1. I HAD to link to an Amazon account. I didn’t want to use mine, as all my info would be available to her, so I set her up a dummy account... however, I couldn’t download even free apps because I didn’t link a payment method to her new account, and there was no way to sign into my Prime Video account unless I linked my entire account to her Fire Stick.2. ADS. What the heck are all these ads? Not to mention all the videos advertised on the home page that you may or may not have access to through an app—otherwise you have to pay for.3. It was VERY easy to stumble on something that you would have to pay extra for, AND the payment pretty much would just go straight through upon clicking it. There wasn’t any “are you sure” or discussing payment options—which meant it was very easy to accidentally click on something and end up shelling out money for it by mistake. Which would NOT work for her. I ended up accidentally buying something myself trying to figure it out.4. I PIN protected in-app purchases thinking that would take care of the issue... it did not. There’s a separate box you have to mark for ANY purchases.5. After PIN protecting purchases, I still had difficulty with the search option on the home page. I could find the shows I wanted to watch, but when I would click to take me there, it wanted my PIN even though the app was already installed, rendering the search feature useless for my purposes.6. Couldn’t get rid of certain apps.7. The remote wouldn’t pair with my TV. Which wasn’t a big deal, I could use the other remote to change the volume and input, HOWEVER, as long as this thing was plugged in, MY TV REMOTE WOULD NOT EVEN WORK. There was no adjusting the volume! No changing the channels! But as soon as I unplugged the fire stick, the remote would work again. (I tried after this with my Roku, and it did the same thing, so it was apparently just an issue with the HDMI ports on this particular TV, but it made the fire stick useless to me anyway.)Overall, even if it had worked as intended and didn’t throw my TV into a fritz, I still wouldn’t have kept it because I didn’t like the interface. Which is something I should have anticipated considering I don’t like Prime Video’s interface to begin with. But it was recommended to me, so I gave it a shot. The one thing I did like was the remote. It was very clicky. 10/10, would recommend the remote.

title: Junk What a tiresome Joke.
body: This is the fourth time I've purchased Firestick. The remote control fails within the first year. Last time, within the first month. It took hours of time texting within remote chat, answering a hundred very stupid questions before I finally got a replacement 'remote control'. We'll see how easy it is to sync it with the firestick. I think more and more, electronic companies are going on the cheap. We're paying top dollar for poorly manufactured materials, and penalized if we don't buy an extended warranty. A product has an implied warranty, that it will last past the value you paid for it. This goes for phones, computers, television sets, etc. I spend more time trying to get problems fixed than the products are worth. Probably not even quality tested before being shipped out in bulk. We deserve better quality for our hard earned money... and not spend as much time with customer service every time a product fails. Sad, really sad. Customer satisfaction is a thing of the past!!!!On a hilarious note, the replacement remote also broke 2 weeks after arrival. I called to tell them it wouldn't turn on the TV or control volume, but it could navigate the Firestick Menu on my television. What do you think was the first question I was asked ? "Have you checked the batteries"? Oh right, a remote with dead batteries can change channels. I finally negotiated a new remote.The shipped the USB connector for the back of the TV instead.Called customer service, and all of the telephone lines are shut down until further notice due to COVID. Can't find a chat. They really just want to prevent people from getting what they pay for, yes? Being able to charge you for junk, making you spend hours on the phone asking for proof of faulty equipment? And then ship you the wrong thing and give no way of contacting them?If there's one piece of legislation I'd like to see passed, wait, two. 1. Every purchased item comes with a toll free customer service number that's at least opened during normal business hours Monday through Friday, and 2. That you don't need to sit on hold listening to ridiculous promotional recordings that you probably have to listen to anyway, even if a CSR is available. Forced advertising isn't part of customer service. FED UP!

title: Updated review, product is still bigger piece of junk, not worth the plastic it is made of
body: Update May 2020I decided to buy another because I don’t have much options. This one also crashes within 15 minutes. We thought that maybe it was here at home because of internet so we took it to the RV and there is great wifi. Lots of others use the fire stick to watch tv. No problem. No luck. This one still, within 15 minutes crashes and returns to home screen. I figured I’d give it to a neighbor and they returned it the next day. It kept rebooting on them too, they don’t want it. I don’t want it either. Going to try the newest Roku instead. It cannot be worse than this!UPDATE - APR 2020I got an email from someone offering me a $50 amazon gift card to permanently delete this 3 star review. Wow. We have had nothing but problems with the unit. Every single time it is used, either the screen will go to blank black for 2 to 8 minutes, will crash and go back to main menu, will lose internet when the router is 15 away, no sound, or freeze. Every single time is a crap shoot which problem we will have. Some shows allow you to continue where left off, some need to be started from the beginning and some won’t let you fast forward. Even the Roku did not have these frequent issues. To offer a reward for removing a bad review when it is accurate is very bad business. Because now, I have it public. And posted via Facebook, insta, and twitter. We don’t even use the voice remote feature, so this is a colossal waste of money. If you have other options, take it. Don’t buy this unless you must. The product is junk. Seeing the other bad reviews, I get it. To have us delete them because you don’t like what is truthfully said is sad and bad business.**************************Bought this to replace our aging Roku stick. Seemed easy enough to install and use. Interface is faster, which is a great improvement over the (Roku). However, it will reboot constantly back to the home screen. Sometimes 3 to 4 times in a half hour. As you can imagine, this gets old pretty fast. Never had this issue before.While we got it to escape cable, still tons of ads in programs. Feels like we are in Demolition Man movie. I guess that’s our future. Welcome to 1984.

title: disappointing thus far
body: I ordered the Fire TV Stick because Amazon Prime will stop streaming via my Sony BluRay in September. I was excited to try the fire stick and the price was excellent. The reviews were also excellent mostly. Unfortunately the fire stick would not connect to my WiFi. I spent MANY hours on the phone/chatting with amazon techs trying to resolve. No luck. Then I spent hours working with Linksys techs on my wireless router trying to connect with the fire stick. It seemed impossible to me that my wireless router was able to support my bluray, my Xbox, my cellphones, several smart TVs, an Echo, yet not be able to connect to the fire stick. We decided to buy a new linksys router. We also ordered a replacement fire stick.I hooked up the new wireless router and tried the original fire stick. It connected immediately. So it cost me $125 to get a new wireless router so I could use the fire stick. The replacement fire stick connected as well, so there was nothing wrong with the original one.Then last night when we were streaming amazon prime via the fire stick, we discovered that Closed Captions were delayed by many seconds making CC more of a nuisance than the helpful aid it should be. We enjoy foreign films and shows and many times accents make it hard to hear words clearly so we use CC all the time.We have been streaming both Netflix and Amazon Prime for many years via our Sony BluRay and have NEVER had delay problems with Closed Captions.Right at this moment I am so disappointed with all the problems I have had with the fire stick that I can only give it 1 star. I had to replace my wireless router at a significant cost even though my old one supported ALL the devices in my house, as I mentioned earlier. And now the delayed Closed Captions problem is maddening. From what I have researched, there may be no fix for that. And I am not looking forward to spending any more of my time talking with Amazon techs.I know there are many people who love the fire TV stick. I wish I was one of them. I can't give up because I must find a way to stream Amazon Prime by September 26, when they will know longer stream thru my BluRay.

title: Have had 4 fire sticks stop working for me this year... are they really such a great value?
body: Now one of the fire sticks was the first one I bought 3 or 4 years ago. That was totally worth it for 35 dollars but since then I think Amazon has designed these to fail within a year so you have to keep re-buying them every year or half year. So the first stick started going really slow where it took forever to boot up and couldn't play videos so I happily replaced it at the beginning of the year. Well not too long after getting that one I was having to replace the batteries in the remote every few days! So that one got moved to the bedroom where I had to take out the batteries of the remote every time I was done using it. So foolish me bought another one for the living room. This one started not being able to connect to the internet after a few months and the device would restart on its own sometimes right in a middle of a show or sometimes when you were searching. This got progressively worse over half a year to the point where it would just restart every time you tried using it. So even more foolish me buys another one. After a few months the same thing happened and I said to myself I will never use a fire stick again. Buying 4 sticks a year just isn't worth it. So now we had no way to watch prime even though I had 4 fire sticks and 2 TVs in my possession. Then my mom says well I'm not using my stick so you can have it. OK since its free I guess I'll try it. Guess what the thing restarts randomly just like the rest of them and just like the rest of them it is happening more often the more I use it. Amazon should be absolutely embarrassed to put out such an inferior faulty product. The only reason this sells, and the only reason I bought it, is because Amazon's name is on it. I hate Apple more than any company in the world but in this case I am going to buy an apple TV over a dozen fire sticks.... It just doesn't make sense to replace a stick every half year as I did for both my TVs this year. I have so many fire sticks laying around my 2 year found one and started playing with it as a toy, he uses it like a taser to "shock" me haha. I think I have had more fun with it that way then using it for its intended purpose.

title: Glitchy, laggy, awful, predownloaded crap takes up memory you don’t have. 2 thumbs down.
body: I ordered this product roughly two weeks ago. A couple days after my purchase I checked shipping status to find that UPS had destroyed the entire package to the point it had to be returned to the warehouse. After numerous calls I finally got it sorted out & got another one sent out. So a week later it finally arrives, I’ve never owned a FireStick & have had nothing but problems with my Rokus so I was very excited to try it out. Minutes after setting it up I immediately regretted my decision to purchase this FireStick. I don’t know if they’re all this unbearably laggy & glitchy or if I just got a lemon. It’s at least 10x slower than my roku on all occasions. I’ve had this product in my possession for all of maybe 30 minutes at this point. In those 30 minutes I have received an error stating my internet connections been lost & I need to restart my modem & router at least 20 different times, then seconds later it leaves the error screen & goes to the network connection screen where it states my internet connection status is “Very good” as you can see in the pictures I’ve attached. I can barely even use the FireStick or try to explore the user interface because the network error constantly pops up & forces you to leave whatever you were doing beforehand to deal with the connection issue which it then tells you is fine & you have to go back & start what you were doing all over again. I’m going to give this product a chance for a couple more days & if these issues persist I’m returning it immediately. I’ve never been so disappointed in an amazon product before, specially considering everything I had to go through just to even get the package delivered without begging destroyed first. I don’t care for this product at all, it’s too cluttered & crammed with predownloaded crap that amazon put on that I have absolutely no interest in & is now taking up space on the very limited 5GB of memory I have & nothing I do gives me the option to actually delete these unwanted apps. It’s almost as bad as when Apple forced everyone to download that god awful U2 album that literally NO ONE cared about. Amazon, you should’ve done better with this one. Major flop.

title: Thank you for making my life a living hell, Amazon. FireTV stick should be set on fire.
body: It's been one year and I have five FireTV Sticks in the house and all they do is confuse my poor old 79-year-old mother-in-law who lives with us. DirecTV was a lot easier. My major complaint is that the interface that the user sees is confusing for my MIL. While my adult children and my wife and I can manage through using the Fire stick, my MIL is constantly picking out the apps instead of going into the Hulu app to find shows that she wants to watch and inadvertently selects and loads PAY apps. Today she ordered the PBS app (at $2.99 a month) instead of going into Hulu to find some of the PBS shows she's been watching. During one month, she ordered about 7 pay apps and I had to go into my Amazon account and deactivate a pile of subscriptions (over $30 a month) and then go back to her and show her , "THIS IS HOW YOU FIND <xxx> SHOW IN HULU!". She accidentally orders PAY movies from Prime, and not fully understanding not everything comes standard with Prime. She's almost afraid to watch TV any more due to the Fire interface not being simple enough to run. Again it's way more complicated than DirecTV and even worse than regular cable services she's used. She's also tried the voice command/recognition on the remotes and maybe ONCE out of TWENTY attempts to use it she'll get the desired show or movie. It's been awful. I get yells from the other side of the house to go figure out how the heck she can get 700 Club. What is even worse is that we have Hulu for watching local broadcast channels (and I carefully select and pay for the functions we need), but she accidentally selects the local TV station's Fire app and it doesn't work the same way as in Hulu and broadcasts a TON of commercials and confused the hell out of her. FireTV needs to be more simplified and organized and give at least some more OBVIOUS warnings that things she selects MAY NOT BE FREE (or standard with the service). It's NOT simple, it's confusing AF, and my MIL has given me hell, demanding I bring back and reconnect (the outrageously priced) DirecTV system we had before.I gotta live with my MIL, you don't.

title: Done with Prime... not renewing
body: Since I can't give Amazon feedback privately via email, I've decided to include it in my review... Dear Amazon,Thank you for your services over the past decade. It has been a helpfulconvenience over the years for many purchases. However, over the past fewyears, I've found myself increasingly disappointed with the quality ofAmazon products (music app, Kindle, Fire Stick). In addition I've hadseveral delivery and technical website issues while you continue to make itmore difficult to get in-touch with someone to resolve problems.On April 18th 2020, I purchased a new Fire Stick Remote. After initialsetup I used my TV remote with the app. Months later I changed TV appsubscriptions and needed the menu button so I attempted to switch back tothe Amazon Remote. After 2 hours of troubleshooting on my own and withcustomer service we concluded it doesn't work. Needless to say I was toldnothing could be done. However, I was offered $15 off my membership which Idid NOT accept. The idea that you would give $15 off membership for a lemonof an item that costs $1 to manufacture, then expect me to buy a new one isa joke. So basically you're turning a customer service opportunity into asmall profit, not cool.Given the current circumstances bundled with the latest episode, I havedecided not to renew my subscription come February 2021. I find it verydisturbing and disappointing that a multi-billion dollar company can't do abetter job of resolving this situation. It's companies like Amazon thatcontinue to cut corners on proper training, don't offer pay equal to workdemand, raise prices while actually delivering less, and would rather spendmoney on image campaigns like going carbon neutral then investing more inemployees and keeping subscription prices down. I know it won't be asconvenient for me to shop elsewhere in the future but I'm not going tocontinue to give money to a company that considers it customers merely atransaction.

title: “UNABLE TO UPDATE your fire tv stick”
body: Worst thing EVER! Had the first fire stick and had it for years then upgraded to the new one with Alexa (which I didn’t need)! Arrived on July 16th and worked well until maybe September/October but I didn’t call until last night because my watchlist was near impossible to access and would error out if selecting one of the shows within the watchlist in “your videos” and then the shows (mostly britbox and Netflix) would slow to almost slow motion and then the voice track would be off and not resolve until you back all the way out and start it over. This would happen 10-13 times during one show! So I call on the evening of December 3to see if the tech folks (who are always nice and knowledgeable) could fix it. So they initiate screen sharing (meaning they are driving my fire stick without me touching my remote) and say they are going to restore factory settings and as we attempt to set it back up I get the error message of “unable to update your Fire TV Stick” which apparently is the so called “kiss of death” to a fire stick based on what I’m reading online! So the tech says he will create a ticket (which means nothing) and after calling 8 more times they offered me 10% off a new one telling me the warranty was up 90 days after purchase. Then they tell me they will give me 50% off a new one but the new one is out of stock so they said I can buy one at the store (target or Best Buy) and they will reimburse the 50% of what I pay and apply it to my amazon account. So I get a survey and they call me cus I was still not happy and call tech department who tells him it’s not going to get fixed so I was right the trouble ticket was a farce! I’m so dissatisfied and disappointed in Amazon. I have two small children and didn’t plan on being without a tv at Christmas time! Grr!One positive is all the people I spoke with were kind and tried to be helpful! They were all very nice but amazon’s policy to not 100% replace the device they broke (when we were screen sharing) is total crap!Thanks for nothing, Amazon! Shaking my head!

title: Read the above.
body: I’m not certain who made it necessary for us not to stream through our Sony Blu-Ray, but we were perfectly happy doing it that way. I was never happy about seemingly being ask to pay for movies which I saw when I was a theater usher back in the 60’s, but that I s another complaint altogether.We reluctantly bought the recommended Firestick from. Amazon and made the switch, paid your price, then realized we had to buy a splitter in order to get our Blu-ray to function. Bought that from you also. I neglected to mention that it took a high school graduate, 3 college graduates, and a15 year old server at our favorite Chinese restaurant to get the battery section of the Firestick remote open.Since installing all of these items per instructions we have experienced the following problems:A) we have tried to watch a 1 hour program and have been kicked off 4 times and still haven’t gotten through the first 15 minutes. We tried another show and got maybe through the credits. This could be an instrument supplier problem.B) Without changing anything we tried again and could not even get Amazon or Netflix to come up.C) we finally did get it to come up but our sound bar will not function.D) we also had a period where no matter what we did we could get no sound whatsoever.I have a question, to what shows and movies I would watch on the old grid we had with Blu-Ray that had a rental charge or PPV charge. On the new grid it does not indicate that. Am I to assume if it does not tell me there is no charge? Please clarify.E) I did watch one movie in its entirety with sound and no interruption when we first hooked everything up.But no more. We have had a friend help us a server and now we are waiting for our internet supplier to come out to see if they are part of the problem.So for 2 weeks we have had no Amazon, Netflix or any other service.I suppose we could cancel our Amazon Prime service but I would hate to deal your company such a blow!Please help us!

title: If you're from outside the US DO NOT BUY! Worst customer service experience!
body: Ok, so I bought a firestick a few weeks ago, when it arrived it took forever to connect to my wifi, after it connected, the device updated itself. Installed Netflix and YouTube (both worked fine), when I updated the Prime Video app, videos did not load, everytime I tried watching a video this message popped up (PLR_PRS_CALL_FAILED). I contacted customer service told them the issue and that I lived in the Dominican Republic and after more that and hour with them they said the product was "defective" and will send me a new firestick. After the new firestick arrived, immediately set it up and tried watching a video on the Prime app and got the same message again. Contacted customer service again and after explaining them everything, they told me that the firestick was designed to work in the US, but not in my region, he did told me there's an "International Version", which I wasn't told about the first time and which is hardly advertised on Amazon. So pissed that the first customer service representative did not tell me this information after telling them I did not live in the USA. It's frustrating that Amazon, a company much bigger than Netflix has these issues and restrictions! With Netflix I can watch it wherever and using what device or app I have with me. After this horrible experience I am cancelling my Amazon Prime membership, will stick to regular shipping and will stick to Netflix which doesn't give me any hassle at all. I even can cast Netflix to my smart TV from the Netflix app on my phone, but can't cast from the Prime app on the phone to the Firestick, what a joke! So if you're someone reading this from outside the US, buy Roku, Chromecast or another device and do not waste your time and money buying this device! So in all, device does not work outside the US, customer service representatives do NOT have a clu, no info on the Amazon website that this device only works on the US and the supposedly "International Version" of this costs way more and you can't be sure if it will work in your region.

title: Buttons Often Unresponsive
body: Well, since it was purchased directly from Amazon, it should have known who I am, but it didn't. Had to do a lot of resetting info and convincing it that I am me. Bought this to replace my old Fire TV Stick because I wanted volume control on the same remote i use for other stuff. Worth it to avoid the need to fumble for a second remote when the volume on one progrm varies dramatically from another. This works well, but other buttons are far less responsive that the buttons on my previous Fire TV Stick remote. Frequently have to push them 3 or 4 times to get a response. Doesn't apper to be a battery issue, but is either a problem with the buttons on the remote itself, or the receiver in the stick itself. No way to really test that, but it's highly annoying. Amazon called while I was editing this review (amazingly quick service) and told me how to reset the remote which I did. Immediate result was I had to punch the button 3 times to get the stick to respond, but then it worked fine. Since the issue was intermittent, I am suspicious that the reset didn't help, but we are in test mode now. She will call back on Tuesday to see if the remote needs to be replaced or if the reset worked. Upping my star rating from 2 to 4 based on the customer service response which is simply amazing. Not a 5 star rating because the audio, while decent, isn't as HiFi as I would like. Same as on the old stick, so that was expected, but perfect it ain't. Well, somebody other than Paula called me back. He offered to replace the unit, but since I live in a remote area, I would have to travel 30 miles to the UPS office which is only open 1 hour a day (4-5) PM and which has refused to accept UPS return numbers from Amazon in the recent past. If I accepted the return, I would not only have to spend an hour or so reinstalling stuff, but as a practical matter have to use the post office for the return which means I would have to cover the shipping costs myself. I truly hate Amazon, though I have no choice but to use them.

title: Piss poor connection on a really strong WiFi network.
body: I’m afraid that this is not worth the 15 bucks I paid for it . it is not often that I can watch an entire show be it a 30 minute show or a full hour show or a movie without having to change networks from my router or restart the fire stick or power cycle my modem . My Roku never once ever required me to restart it or to pick my other network from my router in order to watch a show . The piss poor connection is nonstop continuous and reliable in the sense that I know when I sit down to watch a show I can’t get comfortable because I will have to get up and power cycle my modem . I would like to reiterate that my Roku never once ever required me to change Wi-Fi networks or unplug it in order to watch shows on it. The most frustrating part is hearing people talk about how their fire stick works like a charm that they don’t ever have any issues with it and then I have to contemplate is the $15 I spent on this worth the aggravation to try and get it replaced or repaired. I work from home and my job requires that I have a very strong stable and reliable Internet connection and so I pay the money to have a really good strong and stable Internet connection. And I say that so we can do away with the theory that I have a crappy Wi-Fi signal. There is no interference between the fire stick and my modem. There are no other devices besides my cell phone and my iPad that are sharing that connection. The fire stick has the latest firmware. With all of those conditions met the only thing that I can assume is the quality of manufactu there is no interference between the fire stick and my modem. There are no other devices besides my cell phone and my iPad that are sharing that connection. The fire stick has the latest firmware. With all of those conditions met the only thing that I can assume is that the quality of manufacture is poor. This was a brand new fire stick and not a refurbished one. In all seriousness I wish you the best of luck with your experience .

title: Skip the Fire Stick - updated
body: Bought this only because Netflix & Amazon Prime Video will no longer stream on my TV. This was supposed to be the solution. Meh. My wireless router sits next to the TV, less than 4 feet from the Fire TV Stick, and I haven't watched an entire episode of anything that didn't buffer or reduce the quality of the image. Sometimes the image quality is so degraded (I assume to keep up with less than ideal stream quality), that faces are all a blur of compression artifacts. There's nothing wrong with the TV, everything else looks great. There's nothing wrong with the router, I can stream audio and video several rooms away on phones, on a PlayStation, on computers, and on a Roku TV. This is a sub par device. Stay away.I thought, based on the five star reviews, that I simply got a bad unit, so I complained, and got a replacement. Nope, the new unit buffers and degrades the image just like the last one. So, if I have plenty of bandwidth, no other device in my house buffers when attached via WiFi, no one else was using WiFi because we were all trying to watch Netflix, and the Fire Stick is less than 4 feet from my generally speedy router, it would seem that the wildcard here is the Fire Stick.I wouldn't recommend this device to anyone at this point.Update 3/7/2021I've been suffering with this thing for over a year. Still hate it.I initially thought that it was conflicting with my cable TV. Every time I went from one to the other, the router from FIOS would reset. I would get the "Home is currently unavailable" error, and my router from FIOS would indicate that the internet was being reset.I'm no longer using cable TV, but every time I power on the Fire Stick, the router resets the internet connection. So, it takes 4 or 5 minutes to start watching, and everyone else in the house loses their internet connection. None of the "helpful hints" seem to work.I stand by my "skip this device" opinion.

title: GREATLY LACKING!
body: First of all, I pretty much love my Echo Dot and Echo Inputs. For the most part, with the three devices spread over the apartment I can direct Alexa to perform a task and it's done. There are those times when I'm faced away or the TV is too loud that she doesn't catch the instruction. I consider that reasonable because when someone faces away from me and talks I too have difficulty understanding what was said.However, when the description said Alexa Voice Remote I expected it to be basically a hands free operation like all my other Echo products. But NO! The voice remote requires that I push a button to be able to communicate with the device. The whole point was hands free operation without that I might as well stick to my Roku because I see no benefit to the Fire Stick.For example, I wanted hands free operation because I'm frequently working in my kitchen. My cats are on a raw diet and I make about 30 pounds of food at a time. The undertaking takes up the majority of a day at which time I have on gloves and I'm working with raw meat. It would be really beneficial to be able to say "Alexa, turn on the TV. Alexa, go to Prime Movies, Alexa look for (blank movie), Alexa play (blank), Alexa pause..... And on top of that, the remote won't do what it should! If I want to select an option the remote won't allow me to select something like "Captions". I actually had to call Amazon and they ended up having to do remotely what I should have been able to accomplish myself!However, I still have to press a button to direct her to do anything. And, if I have to press a button I might as well press a button on my ROKU remote. This has been a huge waste of my money and a huge disappointment in a product developed by Amazon. Admittedly, Alexa is not Siri and she still has a lot to learn but marketing this product as a voice enabled product is dishonest and ridiculous!SHAME ON YOU AMAZON!

title: I'm not impressed.
body: It's an overrated product. First of all while the remote control looks sleek and nice opening the battery compartment is an exercise in futility. I had to ask one of my friends for help to open it because if I don't I would've destroyed the remote out of frustration and while he was able to do it he struggled with it too and took about may be 7 minutes to open the damn thing. I don't know why amazon can't make it simple like how any other remote out there where you just open it, put the batteries on and presto you're done. Huge minus points for not being user friendly at all. Second the interface looks nice and clean but most of the shows and features are paid services or if it's depending on the series it's only free for may be one or two episodes. Sorry I lost interest when I have to pull out my credit card. May be there's free stuff in there but I highly doubt I would find anything interesting nor do I have the time to sift around looking for something cool because there's already youtube for that and it's free. And lastly it does have a feature where I kind of use and enjoy a lot which is streaming/casting. I was like cool I got another way to cast a device on my TV. The huge downer is it only works on certain device. Bummer. Doesn't work on PC and doesn't work on any android device except may be a Samsung device. It just sucks that google and amazon don't like each other that's why their product or device doesn't interface at all. I have been interested in the fire stick device for a while and was recommended to me by a couple of friends and a good thing family gifted us one so I don't have to spend money on this device. As of now it's hooked up on my TV and I don't know exactly what to do with it and while google chrome can only do one thing and that's casting it does it well and it's mostly compatible on all three types of devices like apple, PC or android. May be this product is just simply not for me.

title: "TV doesn't support that." Poor integration with TV.
body: Extremely disappointed with this Fire Stick TV. For basic streaming, it suits it's purpose. However, the first disappointment, I'll get into in a sec. The 2nd disappointment is that I have to go through extra steps to try to return it (have to speak to an Amazon support person.. I have prime, I've done my share of returns, never had to do this for anything else. Why make it more difficult for your product, Amazon?)So the major disappointment with it is that it takes over and replaces my current TV integration with my Alexa ecosystem. I have a Sony Bravia TV and have it integrated via Home Assistant. It shows up as Sony Bravia TV, and responds to just "TV." With my original integration, I could control my TV however I want, by voice. Volume level 12? "Alexa, tv volume 12." Instant.With the Fire Stick TV, it adds a new device to my ecosystem named TV. Now, whenever I want to do more than just turn my TV on or off by voice, I get the wonderful "TV doesn't support that" response from Alexa. Why are you going to take over my TV integration and provide minimal functionality? Try to have her switch inputs by voice (it's not on the Fire Stick TV remote)? She either misunderstands the command or "doesn't support that." What, then, is the purpose of configuring controls for my inputs from the Fire Stick TV? The only input control that functions is if I turn on the TV with the Fire Stick TV remote. Then she will turn on my TV and immediately jump to the right input. So she obviously has no problem controlling input selection.I'm sure this won't be a problem for some people, but if I have to use my original TV remote along side my Fire Stick TV remote, just for very basic functionality, it's more hassle than it's worth. And overriding an existing integration just makes it worse. I have an Echo Dot 2, Dot 3, Plus, Show 5, and Show 8. This is by far the most disappointing Amazon device I've used.

title: Very Disappointing
body: So many people convinced me I needed to add the Fire Stick to my entertainment options, but just a day after setting it up I am ready to dump it. After going through the set up, I found a TV series I wanted to watch so I used it to test the system. 50 minutes into the show, Fire Stick lost the connection to the internet. All of my other wifi toys were still working, but Fire Stick could not connect to the internet. This morning I tried to resume play where it left off and couldn't figure out how to "Resume". Turned out it didn't matter, because it lost connection to the internet again and it was 45 minutes before it reconnected.When it did reconnect, I went through and added about 25 or 30 TV series to my "Watch List". I cannot find and have no idea how to access my "Watch List". I've tried through the Fire TV menu and through my Fire TV app, but there is nothing that tells me how to use my Watch List.I cannot figure out how the Fire TV app and the Alexa app are supposed to work. As far as I can see, the Fire TV app allows me to use the return function to go back in my menus, and it will show me a display of my App icons, but I cannot see another thing that it provides. No instructions, no walk through set up, nothing.I think I have the Fire TV added in to my Alexa app, but again, I don't see - and there is nothing that really tells me - how it is supposed to integrate and what I should be able to do. I do see the Fire TV on my list of devices, so I know it's connected through Alexa, but I have no idea what it should be able to do for me.I suppose if the Fire TV won't stay connected to the internet, the integration and supporting apps are useless anyway. UPDATE 3/9/2020: I tried for over an hour tonight to connect and all I could get was the error message in the picture included. It shows I am connected to the network, but it cannot find the internet.

title: Random pausing
body: We decided to cut the cable in July 2020, I did a lot of research in to what was the best streaming system for the lowest possible cost. I read many reviews on Amazon, Cnet, Consumers' Reports etc. Just about any ware I could get information. While it was a hard choice the Amazon fire stick appeared to be the better bet. So I ordered 3 units with two of the Ethernet adapters. Hooked them up and proceeded to add the streaming services we had chosen to replace the channels from Uverse. This is where the problems started occurring there were a number of freezing's, drop outs, and lost signals. I attributed this to weak wifi signals and poor routers. I replaced the routers with no improvement, followed a U tube suggesting about tuning the Firesticks and upgrading the cabling (had cat 5) this improved the issues but did not completely stop them. We enjoy letting OA News to play in the background as we go about our activities, the show would stop and we could not get it running again. One of the suggestions was to unplug the FIrestick to allow the capacitors to cool. This allowed use to restart the program. I also replaced the supplied power supply with a 2 amp unit, this improved performance another notch. Some of the stoppages were removed but there were still a lot. I could not watch Hulu because of the stoppages. Frustrated I decided to try a Roku streaming stick + , once plugged in and set up the streaming services acted as I thought they were supposed to No stoppages, no lost signal beautiful picture. I tried this on a different TV and still had the same results. I than ordered a Roku Ultra LT because I liked the idea I could use the Ethernet. This also worked out well, I was able to stream Hulu without any stoppages. If I could I would return the worthless Firesticks but I am beyond the return window. I would not recommend the Fire TV Stick

title: Absolute Garbage. Don't buy this crap.
body: I have two of these. After about a year of operation, I disconnected one to move the television to which it was attached to another wall. When I reconnected it, the piece of junk does NOT recognize the ethernet connection, and it won't connect to WiFi. I restarted it. I factory reset it. I disconnected it for a minute, 10 minutes, an hour, and a full day. It still won't connect to WiFi and it won't recognize the ethernet connection. A few days later, the TV to which the second Fire Stick was connected died, so I replaced it with a smart TV and moved the second Fire Stick to the other TV. I was able to get it connected and working, so I just wrote the first experience off and didn't think about it much, since I now only needed one. Then, my wife disconnected the ethernet cable from this thing...I now have the exact same d*mn issue with this one that I had with the other. It absolutely will NOT recognize the ethernet connection or connect to WiFi. I've done all the same troubleshooting on this one that I did on the other. I've followed all of the instructions I can find from Amazon. I've tried everything I've found is forums where others have had the same BS problem. All to no avail. I now have TWO Fire Sticks that are completely borked because I did the unthinkable and disconnected them from their ethernet connection.This is quite possibly the dumbest tech error I've ever had. Read other reviews about this piece of crap. This happens far more often than it should. There are dozens, if not hundreds, of reviews on this site that say the same thing. You'd think a company with the financial resources of Amazon could afford to build a device that doesn't go to s*it just because you disconnect an ethernet cable. I'm legitimately considering never buying an Amazon brand product again, because this is absolute BS.

title: Far from refined
body: The fire stick loses connection to amazon servers every couple of days. It also has issues streaming Hulu. This is probably the same issue. Stream quality drops to what seems like 1280x920 about once or twice a day. I dropped directTV and went to this. Not sure who you would call to try and address this issue. I guess that is why you pay twice the fee for traditional cable, someone to talk to if you have an issue. May have to try sling or another service.Update after 7 months. It doesn't drop connection nearly as much, but I have also switched from ATT to Spectrum internet. It does still drop the video quality now and then, and my router is right next to the tv and I have 200G service. I also now have a 2nd gen device on another TV. Figured I would try an upgraded version during the Black July sales event. Now another issue is cropping up with this version that I also forgot to mention in the earlier review and might be related to the signal loss issue. Sometimes when the signal goes out, the only way to get it working again is to unplug the power from the device, and sometimes pull it out from the HDMI slot then reinstall. Luckily it remembers my settings and passcode but getting behind my TV is an ordeal as I'm sure it is with most folks and larger TV's these days.Maybe the newer 4k version has finally got it right. But so far it still isn't great.On a side note, HULU live TV service is not very good. The user interface is bad, using the dvr is a pain, can't get stuff to delete from the online DVR sometimes. Has nothing to do with the Fire stick, just wanted to throw this in.Further update, It also doesn't work well with Disney+ service. Two different tvs with fire sticks buffer Disney+ but it works fine on the PC. Just not cracked up to what it is supposed to be,

title: STICK TO ROKU
body: Absolute waste of my money!!! I purchased this item and it was working fine... until 3 months in it started disconnecting itself every 3 minutes for 4 days straight. I called support and asked if I could get a replacement because clearly this was a faulty device, and not only did the representative not believe me, she was extremely rude saying things things like "that's what I said, isn't it?" and "well maybe that's why you should have bought the warranty." She offered me 10% off a new device, after 3 months of usage. Frustrated I told her that I wasn't going to take a sales tax reduction on a brand new device I had no reason to trust was going to work based on it's history. I told her that given the short amount of usage, and that Amazon has more than enough money to afford a $30 replacement, I would as a loyal prime member, really appreciate a replacement. She finally put me on with a manager after over 1 hour waiting on hold, who agreed to send me a refurbished device and instructed me to keep my remote and wires as they would work with the new plug in. What he failed to explain was that if I did not return the old device in 30 days from receiving the refurbished one I would be charged the full price of a brand new unopened one (what?????). I never sent it because I have neither the car nor the desire to take off work to stand in a post office all day, kept it as backup, went to use when it predictably kept turning off and THE REMOTE WONT RECOGNIZE THE DEVICE!?!?! So its an utterly useless $30 piece of plastic. I have worked in retail both online and in store for 12 years, if someone complains about a faulty product I don't argue, I do my best to accommodate if they are being reasonable. Being able to use my tv streaming plug-in I have now, I guess, paid double for I think is reasonable.

title: What a piece of poo, a new low for Amazon.
body: Giant piece of poo. Ordered on Prime Day. Didn't open it for four months, dead on arrival. Called tech support, no help. Told me it only has a three month warranty, yes your read that correctly, they stand by their product for a grand total of 90 freaking days. Really? 90 Days? That's it?!?!?!After 2 hours on the phone with some dude in India with a fake US name, it was determined mine was defective and he would send me a replacement since although I was beyond the 90 days they could see I never used the thing (kind of scary). Finally I received the replacement yesterday, it was only a stick and I am required to send back the old unit which included the power cord, power adapter, and remote. Really amazon? What am I supposed to do with a stick that has no way to power or operate it? It's not even heavy enough to be a paper weight or door stop. Do you have any other suggested uses for a stick that you have no way to power or interact with?So after my second call and nearly three hours on the phone with Amazon reps they agreed to refund me $15.88 for this stupid thing. So, I spent three hours for nothing. No product and my refund equates to $5.29 per hour spent to receive it. I should have just thrown the piece of crap away when I got it, would have been a much better use of my time.Never again will I ever buy any Amazon branded product again. EVER!Come on Amazon, you are better than this. You could have done the right thing and just sent me a replacement unit in a 10 minute call, but nope you couldn't. Instead you paid some guy and gal more than what the cost of the unit was to give me a hard time about getting a replacement for your defective piece of crap product. Good job Amazon. Well done. Excellent.

title: Prime Day Scam
body: Amazon advertised this same item with two months of HBO free. On Monday July 15th, the first day of Amazon Prime Day the Fire Stick with Alexa voice control and two month of HBO free ( with automatic renewal after the two months, which could be cancelled) was on the website from 12:01 am to around 8:15 am. Problem is when you clicked on the "order button" it would sayfailled and make several attempts and then say attempts failed. then the item was taken off the website. Customer service sait there was an issue with their system and they would put it back on the website when it was fixed. After talking to customer service several times over the two Prime Days on July 15th nd 16th it was never fixed. This item and price had been talked about in advance to "Prime Days" on several electronics websites and even the Amazon website before it was "supposed" to be available to order on the weekend. Since I really wanted the "free HBO for two months" and not the "$15.00 credit per month for 3 months on Sling TV". Sling TV costs $25.00 per month so the $15.00 credit would still cost you $10.00 per month and wouldn'y provide me with anything I don't already have. So I ordered this item to get the Fire Stick for $14.99 and won't activate the Sling TV offer. I already have one Fire Stick and just wanted a second on for another TV. My disapointment is with Amazon who advertised and offered a Prime Deal item which had lots of people who wanted to order it and then never came through. They also never gave an explanation other then "they are working on the issue and with offer the item if the issue is resolved during Prime Days". Which it never was. This was a rotten thing for Amazon to do to it's Prime members and has a negitive effect on their best customers towards the company.

title: They crippled the fire stick with their last "upgrade" can't organise apps
body: They crippled it! U can no longer move apps to where u want. U have to figure out in reverse where u want the apps and pin the one u want the least to the front then unpin it. Then pin the one u want second least to the front and continue till u have pinned the one u want the most to the front. Basically if u want any order to your list of apps and games u BETTER NEVER DOWNLOAD ANYTHING NEW! OR HAVE AN HOUR TO FIGURE OUT HOW TO REVERSE ENGINEER THE ORDER OF YOUR APPS 1 AT A TIME AND PRAY U DONT MAKE A MISTAKE AND HAVE TO START ALL OVER AGAIN! I would have never kept it if I knew they were going to cripple the system right after it was too late to return it. Imagine having a book shelf and in order to put it in alphabetical order u needed to use a VERY clumbsy robot arm to put Z first then work backwards till z was finally in last place, except instead of alphabetical order u need to figure out what apps u think r most important in reverse. It's litterslly the stupidest thing I've seen any company call an upgrade I can ever remember ever. I can not think of a bigger screw you to the users if they simply want to put tubi in front of crackle or something and need to go from switch 2 objects mode to practical astrophysics string theory levels of on the fly reverse mental organisation. Maybe there is some written algorithm u can figure out on paper but they turned switching on a light with a switch to rebuilding a house from scratch every time u want to flip the switch! This is absolute bull ****!!! I would kick the **** out of everyone responsible for this if i knew I was in line with them at the store or something!! I'm not buying anything off Amazon aside from what my elderly neighbor needs until this fist up my *** has been pulled out!

title: WHY DOES AMAZON THINK WE WANT MORE TRAILERS & COMMERCIALS ???
body: If you'd enjoy paying $15.00 for ROBO CALLS, this is for you ! As a dedicated Prime member, I DO NOT WANT TO SEE MOVIE TRAILERS FOR OTHER MOVIES THAN WHAT I'M ABOUT TO WATCH ! On Good Omens I couldn't watch a trailer of the show itself, but in Roku 2nd gen I could. Every detail is HALF-BAKED ! Also the Prime t.v. interface sucks. There is no banner showing which movies are free on Prime. You can't scroll sideways to the next video, but need to go down to view cost etc then up and over to see the next choice and so on... I really wish Amazon would put in the extra time during tech development for their devices to work better. This seems to be a consistent problem with anything that is Amazon technology. Also on watching YouTube, you get extra commercials. On 10 minute videos, I get the commercial in the beginning, then 2 more during the video ! I tested the same videos on Roku, and I only get the one in the beginning. Whether it's Amazon or the YouTube app itself this REALLY SUCKS ! Also every YouTube video you hover over plays a video loop of 3-5 seconds WASTING PLENTY OF DATA ON VIDEOS I'M NOT EVEN INTERESTED IN WATCHING ! DUH ! This device has limited memory, people complain they need to dump the data too often. Also for the sake of a greener world, we don't need to waste electricity and data and more servers on useless crap ! This is BACKWARD THINKING AND DESIGN, ALL OF IT !!! ISN'T THE PURPOSE OF TECHNOLOGY TO IMPROVE OUR LIVES ? The only improvement over Roku, is the better responding remote. That is the reason I bought this. But the better remote does not make up for the flagrant flaws which are deal breakers for me ! THIS DEVICE DOES NOT IMPROVE MY LIFE ! THIS ONLY ADDS MORE AGGRAVATION !

title: Can We Watch Something?
body: So I bought this back at the end of May in 2019. It only lasted about 7 months in usage. Heres what the problem was:1) in about January I noticed the device would automatically go to a black screen and go through what seemed to be a reboot/ update..and it did this a couple times but I didnt think much of it and just didnt use the stick for about a month to month and a half.2) after that month to month and a half I wanted to use it, but it constantly kept doing this black screen issue with the amazon and fire tv logo in an endless cycle.3) i called amazon and they walked through the steo of force stopping SOME apps and clearing the cache, which was useless because the person who tapped into my stick was clearing cache for apps that had ZERO cache.3) called back to say that didnt work and now that the person had me factory reset it, it keeps telling me that it cant update so now I'm technically locked out of the device because it wont proceed any further.4) called back and blah blah blah, in the end the person I spoke to said they normally only give 15 percent off but because of this issue they will give 50 percent off a new order of the voice active remote fire stick or the 4k version.5) I get it, that's nice. But my problem is that in my order history the item is listed as "May be possible [to replace item] through May 2020". When I asked about this, the rep said the warranty expired back in October! Then why the hell even say it's a possibility for A year later after purchase when it was only truly valid for 5 months and 4 days!? Not sure I want to buy another stick, I think people should pass on this and go with a Roku. I have a 5 year old roku that has never given me issues

title: Update: Unusably Slow and Freezes Often
body: Update 3/10/20:This Fire TV has so many frequent issues loading that I can’t use it anymore. I think the problem is how it deals with heat—after being on for an hour or two, it heats up and stops functioning properly. It often pauses in the middle of playing a video and requires a complete restart to get working again. I’ve removed it from my TV and gone back to my old device. I wouldn’t recommend that anyone purchase this until Amazon releases an updated version, this version is completely gimped.———Original review:As a longtime owner of Amazon Fire TV - 1st Generation, I upgraded to this new Fire TV Stick on Prime Day for one reason: the improved universal remote. And it's true the new universal remote is great! It controls my TV, sound bar, and Fire TV stick all in one. However, this Fire TV Stick itself loads extremely slowly and is very unresponsive compared to my old 1st gen Fire TV, which is 5 years old!Let me explain the lag issues with this Fire TV stick. When pausing videos in HBO Go or Netflix, the video will freeze quickly, but the audio will continue for 5-10 seconds afterward. Then, when resuming playback, the video will fast-forward for 5-10 seconds without audio until everything is synced back up. Apps crash much more often, too. Menu navigation feels clunky and app load times are longer. Basically, this device is completely gimped compared to its FIVE year old predecessor. For that, I'm hugely disappointed.I wish Amazon would make the new universal remote compatible with older Fire TV devices, so we could get the best of both worlds. However, the improved remote is canceled out by the underpowered FireTV stick here--I wouldn't buy this again.

title: Spotty
body: So after owning this for a few months, I thought I should write a review about it's performance.I had the previous version of the fire stick and decided to upgrade because of the remote having control of the tv to power on/off and adjust the volume. That has been a welcome improvement and I love reducing the number of remote controls I have to look for.On the downside though.The wireless connection is spotty and sometimes nonexistent. I regularly have issues where the wireless drops out while I’m watching a program. Sometimes I have to unplug it and let it restart to get a connection going again. Before you ask, it’s not my wifi. I have other devices on the same WiFi, and they never have that issue. I even bought a WiFi extender to improve the signal and still have this issue. In fact, the only thing in my house that has WiFi problems, is the fire stick!Also, it has an issue with freezing up and getting sluggish. Sometimes when I hit the Alexa button on the remote, it can take up to 10 seconds to respond (yes, I’ve timed it).Overall, I love the fire stick, but the issues with WiFi connectivity and sluggish response, can drive me insane sometimes!Update:Spoke with technical support about the WiFi connectivity issues and basically got no help.Can’t replace it, because it’s out of warranty and the best advice they had was to reset to factory default. This has not helped and the issue with the WiFi continues.I’m now changing my rating to 1 star because of this. If you make a product, you should stand by it. I’ve had it for only 5 months and it becoming useless.I think I’ll switch to AppleTV, at least I know they stand behind their products for a year!

title: Disobedient Device That's Great at Wasting Your Data and Serving You Ads
body: Pros: Easy setup, affordableCons: Doesn't stop streaming when you tell it to, doesn't turn off when you tell it to, forces you to navigate past ads to get to your content, invites a spy into your home, and can't be controlled with infared universal remotes.Like so many modern devices the Fire Stick will do what it's makers want it to, not what you tell it to. When you're done watching content it will continue streaming indefinitely. If you have satellite internet with limited data as i do, this is a disaster. it can literally use up a month of data in a day. If you push the power button on the remote to turn it off, it won't turn off. It's still on and streaming. If you have a 'smart' tv as i do, that means when you turn on the tv and select another input to watch a dvd, the 'smart' tv will switch back to the fire stick input because there's still content being streamed there. if you have a harmony universal remote as i do, you won't be able to control the fire stick with it. When you go to the fire stick home page to pick your content the top item will be an ad that's hard to navigate past. i watched a shameless ad in which i have zero interest three times before i figured out how to kind of double click the down direction on the control to get past the ad without launching it. BTW, i never selected the ad. It launched as soon as it was highlighted. And, of course, if you activate the microphone on the remote (i never did), amazon is listening. and if you don't, it will keep nagging you to turn on alexa.UPDATE: 03/10/21:and now there's a huge hot pink banner ad right in the middle of the home page advertising some phone company (one i'll be sure to never use).

title: Remote unfriendly to elderly and disabled individuals
body: When I found out my elderly parents were paying $300/month for cable TV (apparently they called their cable company for service and magically ended up with extra boxes and extra services even on non-functional TVs), I decided to buy them 3 Firesticks, pay for a streaming subscription, and flew down to Florida to hook them up. After a week of attempting to train them on how to navigate their new service which included buying them large screen TVs to enable easy on-screen navigation along with a cheat sheet in 20 font, they had much difficulty remembering which buttons on the remote to press for navigating. I then followed up by buying 3 silicone neon-colored remote covers at Best Buy to contrast and amplify the remote buttons hoping that would make it easier. The poor things still have much difficulty watching their favs. The problem is that as a result of early memory issues and diminishing eyesight, they constantly have to look at the tiny remote to find out which button to press, at which point they lose their place on the screen. Memorizing left, right, up, down, back, enter etc. is hard for some individuals over 75 particularly with the issues noted. Furthermore, the selected apps are not large or bright enough to help them figure out their location on the screen which causes them to enter the wrong apps subsequently causing mass confusion. I understand Amazon wants to have this sleek, sexy, barely-there remote design that represent 21st century technology, but would it hurt to have an alternate /larger remote design WITH letters/words for elderly and vision-impaired individuals? After all They are perhaps the most constant watchers of TV. Pretty Please?

title: Don’t waste your time and money :(
body: We were excited to get the Firestick to expand our viewing options past what our sony ps3 was allowing us to watch via its available apps. Needless to say, we were sorely disappointed. It took about 45 minutes on the phone with tech support to get the Firestick to even connect to WiFi and for the past 2 weeks we have battled keeping it connected. It is the only item in our home that continually drops signal despite being only 15 ft from the router. The PS3, multiple phones and PCs all do great. From reading other reviews this seems to be a common problem. It’s a shame a company as big as amazon can’t figure out a way to accomplish a simple task like staying connected to WiFi. This is a very basic function of any internet enabled device.I started the return process tonight because it’s very frustrating to come home from work only to spend 2 hrs trying to reconnect to our router 15 times just to be able to “relax” and watch a show. We will be going back to using the PS3 for viewing. In addition to this problem which basically renders the Firestick a paper weight, there are also many software issues. For instance, you press the Alexa button and say “search YouTube for helicopter videos” and it replies by asking which app you want to use to search YouTube for helicopter videos and then displays options like Netflix, amazon prime, and of course YouTube. Forcing you to click and select which app you want to use to perform the search. If I ask for it to search YouTube that should be a given!In my opinion this is just another overpriced piece of amazon junk that didn’t get properly tested before release.

title: This is just OK
body: This is a BIG change from the Roku Express. I had to replace the one I had in my living room and the Fire TV Stick was a lot cheaper than the ROKU Stick so I decided to give it a try. First I have to say that I was disappointed that it doesn't have the Spectrum app available. That is a real problem for me as I only have that for cable. Fortunately that app comes with my Samsung Smart TV.
