The reason I have a Fire Stick is to get the HGTV app, DIY app, BYU app, IDGO app, and all the other apps that are not available on my TV, that are available with the Fire Stick, thank goodness or it would have been a complete waist of money!! I am also not thrilled with the playback quality of the picture. I think the ROKU offered a much clearer and consistent picture. I will live with the Fire Stick for now, but it is going to take a LOT of getting used to. Hopefully, I will like it better as I use it more, and not have to go back to the ROKU. My opinion is if you are looking for the better of the two, spend the extra money and get the ROKU Express... the ROKU Stick overheats!!!UPDATE:Absolutely disgusted with this device!! I have changed my rating to one star, as the thing keeps losing the internet connection for no reason! Besides that, I have to keep restating the device because I get an error message that it can't play my video. Sometimes, it even stops or skips. Rubbish! I would return it if it were cost effective to do so. I will definitely be replacing it with a ROKU EXPRESS... should have saved the headache and ordered that in the first place. At least that has the Spectrum App!!!!!

title: Unstable streaming experience
body: I was very excited to get this device. I had heard lots of great things. I already have a ROKU but wanted an upgrade. At first it was pretty cool. I like the integration with amazon prime, search functions better than ROKU and several other improvements (voice remote is cool). Not long after I got it, It started to malfunction. It would enter a boot loop where it just rebooted repeatedly. I was able to work through it with a factory reset and things were ok for a bit. It's temperamental and needs its provided accessories so don't loose your cable, brick or extension cable. After some extensive research on a resolution, It seems the Amazon FireTV Stick experiences boot loops that make it unusable FREQUENTLY. There are many videos and tips and tricks on YouTube and tech forums describing complicated ways not to loose all of your data once faced with this event. But really the only way to fix it is reset to factory... and your remote has to connect long enough to the device to allow the button combination to register for that menu option to display (mine did not this last time). The only change I made was connected to a new wifi network.If you choose to purchase. Note there is only a couple month limited warranty and Amazon will NOT replace your device. So If it starts to do this boot loop, call the first time. Otherwise, You'll have to pay (with a small discount) for a new one. (I will not be utilizing the discount offered by service)ROKU on the other hand, has given me zero issues. I plug it in and watch! I have not had to spend hours troubleshooting it.

title: Nothing but problems
body: [[VIDEOID:d9c7c1fce1a8aab0c4e5a4b785d0dd83]] We bought the Fire TV stick a little over a year ago. Being able to watch streaming services without using a computer or console connected to the TV is pretty great, but a few days in, we started having problems. Sometimes the remote doesn't work even with fresh batteries, sometimes the fire stick crashes unexpectedly, sometimes the audio is not synced properly with the video, sometimes it sounds robotic, sometimes it boots to a black screen and I have to unplug it and plug it in again to get it to work. Often, it just freezes and I have to start the painstaking process all over again. Eventually, we just stopped using the Fire remote entirely because it was just as reliable as the stick. In fact, it's come to the point where every time we turn the fire stick on, we expect something to go awry. If it doesn't, it's a treat! Yes, I've factory reset the device, yes, I've swapped the batteries in the remote, yes, I've even checked the solder within the remote. I've tested different power sources: the USB on the TV, the AC cable that comes in the box, different outlets...It seems to be a software problem that's beyond my ability to repair. It's only gotten worse over time.I'm technically savvy; I used to use a wireless HTPC setup to the TV, but was hoping that getting a fire stick would make it easier for my wife. I enjoy other Amazon products, but the reliability of this particular device is awful. I have never owned such a poor performing device.I warn you, dear reader: GET SOMETHING ELSE!

title: to good to be true
body: A small device that does a million things for $30? Yeah sounds great. I bought it to replace my Apple TV 3, and, well, you get what you pay for. My biggest gripe is that this thing keeps randomly going to a BLACK SCREEN, requiring me to manually unplug it so it restarts, then go back to what I was doing. Yep, no reliability. Very disappointing. All the bells and whistles and what not -- I'd give them up for just simple reliability. My kid watches a show and I have to deal with the device freezing up once a week. On top of that, it has a lot of WiFi connection issues that I never experienced with my appletv 3. NOT READY FOR PRIME TIME!***UPDATEAmazon sent me a replacement that is stuck in a loop of registration — can’t even get to the main screen to use it. Just loops around to choosing my language, WiFi, register, over and over again. BEWARE THIS DEVICE IS A FRUSTRATION FEST!UPDATEgot my replacement fire stick set up, but the frustration continues. The stick has constant connection issues with a “spinning wheel.” Nothing like settling down to watch something and getting constantly interrupt by the device not working properly. Terrible experience.**additionally, I’ve discovered that Disney Plus does not play back reliably on the fire stick, and Netflix on the fire stick will randomly turn on subtitles. The Fire Stick feels like an unfinished product — unreliable and frustrating.UPDATEFinally gave up on this, returning it, bought a Roku. MUCH better, reliable, and WORKS.

title: Not worth the effort or money
body: I bought the Gen2 Firestick TV to replace my Roku. I'm one of those that will not give up, I'll work all day on a problem to find out what is happening. I just have to now why... Big mistake! The Firestick would not stay on at first, it would just randomly reboot. After much research and studying, I find that the power source is not efficient for the Firestick--does not supply enough power. Everyone says to try a phone charger which provides more power. To my surprise, it worked. I used my Iphone charger with the Firestick USB cable and no more reboots. OK, all is good now, right. NO!Now the back button on the remote stops working and without it, I can't exit certain screens. I have to start using the Home button and start over. Again I do research and find that a reboot of the Firestick usually fixes the problem, but only temporally. So no solution found. I call Amazon support and they tell me to change the battery in the remote. My first thought was, that has nothing to do with it, every thing else is working fine on the remote. I didn't give my option and tried new batteries. That did not work as expected, still no back button. Today I go online to return the Firestick and find out that my time to do a return expired 4 days ago. I waited to long and spent to much time finding solutions to Amazon's Firestick, which they should have done before putting these on the market. Now I'm switching back to my Roku and I guess the Firestick is going into the trash, it's no good with out the back button.

title: worthless
body: We have purchased multiple firesticks for our TV's over the last few years. They get worse all the time. They break easy. They freeze and you think, they are broken. They cannot call up CBS ALL ACCess well. Yes, CBS ALL ACCESS made changes to their menu ,but firestick cannot seem to work anymore. HOweve, Roku works fine with CBS. The firestick keeps loading and looking and dropping our HULU shows. None of this is our Wifi. I can go into another room further from my router, turn it on, change the input to HDMI, start Streaming with my Roku and everything works, while firestick cannot seem to stay connected to the better TV in the room closer to the Wifi. So annoying, We have unplugged, restarted the router etc, until we used the Roku in the other room. We have a second home and the same thing happens. Firestick is trash, we even purchased a warranty plan so we can get up to 2 new firesticks during the warranty. I guess that tells me how bad firesticks are. My next manuever to prove that the firestick is the problem will be to connect the existing Roku's and firesticks to the opposite TV's to prove that the issues are the firesticks. Amazon, you can certainily do better and all these great ratings have to be bogus. Our first firestick worked pretty well until we dropped it a couple years ago. Since then we have replaced our firesticks multiple times and still have problems with brand new remotes. WE only had to replace one Roku that we had for about 4 years after a grandchild dropped it on tile.

title: Works fine until you need tech support
body: Was working fine until it started having issues with streaming. It would lag and buffer after 5 minutes then just stall. ISP was fine, even did a VPN to test it. It was obvious the stick was having issues with download speeds. 2 factory resets, 3 regular resets, and various "troubleshooting" from people who have no idea how the device works.... I still have it.Tech support rundown: The hourly cost Techs Mark, Calidoss, and Lorenzo (that, literally, asked me the same thing over the course of two separate conversations that lasted about 3 hours each) clearly cost more than the $30 of the FireStick itself. Also, for some reason, when you answer a question, they take 3 - 5 minutes to wait to respond.Thanks for making me feel like a POS because I bought stuff from Amazon and needed tech support.**Additional information**I kid you not, they just sent me an email telling my to factory reset my FireStick after I told them I had factory resent it twice. When I responded saying "no" that email didn't help, they refereed me to calling tech support or chatting with them again.... Almost as if this is a sick $^!&ing joke to them.Another update. They had me send them my old firestick in the mail. 5 days later they send me one back that was worse. The video explaining the firestick wouldn't even load and the remote had a 2 second lag. I threw everything against the wall and bought a Roku. I will now forbid all 43 of my properties to buy this thing from Amazon.

title: Bait and Switch on Amazon costing us.
body: I'm writing this because I have Amazon everything, maybe not this particular item. But then again probably. I will be posting this on Amazon items as I will all "Deal of the days" to make sure corrections are made either for myself or for all on delivery promises not met anymore. At all. title: Do NOT Unplug Fire Stick From HDMI Port
body: This Fire Stick was purchased in July 2019. We have had various issues with streaming during these two years. A lot of waiting for it to engage which seemed like buffering.We got fed up with not being able to consistently stream so I called Amazon. The technician had me power off my modem for 15-20 minutes and remove the fire stick. After that he said to power up the modem, wait for the system to come back up, and plug the fire stick back in without using the adapter.Here’s where the problem starts. The Amazon Fire Stick will NOT go back into the HDMI port. The adapter will not go into the port either. The fire stick is the only thing that has ever been plugged into the HDMI port.I had a technology specialist come look at the HDMI and the fire stick. He was unable to get the fire stick into the port. He also tried the adapter and it would not work either. He tried the fire stick in another HDMI and again it wouldn’t go in.We Googled the issue for a possible work-around and it seems as though this is a very common problem.I called Amazon and spoke with Keith. He offered me a 15% discount on a new fire stick. I declined and told him I wasn’t going to buy a new one because the one I have has been plugged in for two years and should be operable.Try to find another way to fix your issue by NOT removing the Amazon Fire Stick from your HDMI port. We are now stuck with no ability to stream!

title: Back to the drawing board
body: In the last couple years, we have bought six Fire tv Sticks, most of them from Amazon. We severed our relationship with Spectrum 3 months ago, but we're not happy with Hulu + Live tv and will quit soon to try other services. The Fire tv Stick has the following problems:1. Curved back; it rocks if you try to push a button or the directional wheel (we have them sitting on desks next to our mouse pads); have to hold it with both hands or hold the edges with fingers, reserving one digit to push the buttons/wheel; ridiculous!2. Buttons are hard to see in low light; no back-lit feature.3. There's no keyboard; yes, that would take a lot of room, but even a number pad or a few function keys would allow the cable services to offer more features (such as return to previous channel!); the remote that came with my small Samsung tv is only about 50% larger and has EVERYTHING!! There's no good reason for trying to make the Fire tv Sticks smaller and smaller.4. I've had an Echo for several years, but I will never use it to give instructions to the Fire tv Stick; this is a large house, and the Echo is downstairs (and no, I don't wish to by any Echo dots); I would not like hollering down to Alexa from where I watch tv upstairs; plus it might impact the tv settings of the rest of my family.5. The mute button needs to be larger and more prominent; it needs to be farther away from the volume up/down toggle.We will not buy any more Fire tv Sticks.

title: If your Fire TV Stick  stops working well, call Amazon ASAP!
body: Worst piece of garbage ever! I’m assuming mine is defective so, after much argument with Amazon, they are replacing it. We bought the Fire TV stick & it worked for about a week. Since then it has been hit or miss. What happens? It is constantly crashing & rebooting!!! We’ve done ALL the recommended Amazon fixes. We’ve purged the app caches. We’ve deleted & reinstalled the apps. We’ve even done the factory reset of the stupid thing 5 TIMES now! I called Amazon to let them know & ask for a replacement. THEY SAID THEY WOULD SEND A REPLACEMENT FOR $15!!! That’s quite a scam, send a defective product and rather than exchanging it outright because it doesn’t work, charge your long-time Prime members that pay $120 a year just to be a Prime member $15 to replace the defective product they bought from you. I unfortunately called after 45 days. They say they have a 30 day replacement Guarantee. Wow... 30 days! Way to stand behind your own products Amazon. I am now expecting my replacement to arrive today. Hopefully this one will work. I’m sure their tech support doesn’t want to hear me say, “It’s crashed. And now it’s rebooting.” ever again.Imagine if every product was this quality... Driving on the highway your car engine just stops randomly. Calling on your cell phone & it reboots every call & you have to dial again. Having to unplug & plug your refrigerator back in every 15 minutes because you are trying to make ice in the freezer. You can do better Amazon!

title: Garbage
body: Absolutely terrible. I own the last version of the Fire TV Stick and it is great. This new version is terrible. The remote does not work most of the time. I literally have to slam the thing on the table to make it work, and it will only work for a few button presses until stops again. Tried new batteries, paring and unpairing, nothing fixes it. The actual Fire Stick itself is also terrible. It is slow, lags, and crashes all the time and I have to unplug the power to reset it. I don't know why this version is so bad, because my old Fire Stick has worked fine for years. I tried to exchange this for a new one, but I received it for free when I signed up for SlingTV (they were having a promotion and gave me a coupon code to get it from Amazon) The Fire Stick shipped directly from Amazon on my account. So when it didn't work I asked Amazon to replace it. They however will not just replace or let you exchange something. Amazon will only do a refund not a replacement for Fire Sticks, and since the order was for $0 after using the SlingTV coupon my refund would be zero lol. So what is the point of that??! Return it and get nothing back? I have a useless device that is worth nothing and you wont replace it. At least it was "free". However it really wasn't free, because the SlingTV promotion was for two months of service free OR a free Fire Stick. Guess I should have gone with the two free months! So thanks Amazon and SlingTV, nice scam you have going.

title: Garbage
body: I have had this for a while now it worked well at first it is not something that has a long working life. It works when it wants it’s Slow as #@ you have to turn it on a while before you want to watch something it takes that long. My other bigger complaint is the Amazon support or lack of with their fire stick issues. Up until recently I was able to at least have Alexa from my app or any device turn the tv on or off. That stopped so they had me unregistering and reregistering it that did nothing. I did a update still nothing, I can add it to my devices but that’s it it’s Just in a list. Alexa says device or group not found. I was on line chatting with 5 or more CS reps they couldn’t help. I then placed a call on the phone 3 hours he couldn’t figure it out I was supposed to get a call back never did. Now if I call again I don’t have a event or case number so I have to start All Over explaining everything that was done. I have a newer 4K fire stick and newer cube that so far is working. I guess because this is a much older version they don’t last and they are you feel like you need to buy a newer one.Thanks AmazonI since have purchased a new one so it went from Bad To Worse Now the remote keeps un syncing from the stick. The when I get it sync it doesn’t do everything! Why do I keep wasting my money on these products. Time to just get all smart TVs so I never need a Fire anything at that time I can put my firestick in The Actual Fire

title: Nothing but problems so far -- frequent disconnects from wifi; remote problems
body: I've been a long time user of Chromecast. I saw the Firestick on sale and grabbed one because Amazon doesn't play nice with Google and it's difficult to broadcast Prime Videos on Chromecast. I don't know if I've gotten a few duds or whether this is just a poorly designed product, but I've had nothing but problems. When I receive the device it was pretty easy to set up, but from the get-go it was constantly disconnecting from wifi. My Chromecast is plugged into the same TV and streams everything perfectly.After contacting support, they decided to send me a new one as they thought may be there were problems with the hardware. However, they sent the new one without a remote. When I tried to set it up, it couldn't pair with my original remote. After spending more time with support they're now sending a replacement remote.For everything Amazon does well, they I don't think they do hardware pretty poorly. I didn't like the Alexa that much and ended up returning it, and have had nothing but problems with Firestick. Of course, if they'd just let Chromecast stream Prime Video I wouldn't have to go through this at all.The one bright spot is the customer service agents I've chatted with. The customer support chat is a great option, and they're willing to send replacements in hopes I get lucky with a piece of functioning hardware. While there is some good Prime Video content, I'm not sure it's worth all this effort.

title: 5 things the Fire TV stick got wrong
body: First let me say... I really WANTED to love this product but unfortunately, I don't think Amazon completely understands the user experience side of their devices. (Surprising considering they try so hard with things like alexa.) We have echo dot alexa devices in almost every room of our home and I so wanted the freedom of being able to use our echo to control the TV. Our Roku TV and Roku Sticks were somewhat limited in the voice command area so despite my long term loyalty to the Roku Brand, I purchased 3 fire TV sticks for our bedroom TV's.In the master bedroom, the fire stick is passable and even conveinent at times. I can launch specific shows by saying things like "Alexa Play Bones on prime video." I can pause, rewind, skip episodes etc.HOWEVER, that's where the excitement stops. I hate to say it, but that's the only positive thing I can say about the firestick. But I do have plenty of Negatives, many of which make the device impossible/dangerous for child use. I've listed the ones that I believe are deal-breakers.1. Constantly freezes, resets, loads, etc.I had watched several Youtube video reviews that showed how fast the firestick was in comparison to the Roku and I can now tell you that those reviewers were either lucky, given a superior product, or complete fiction.We've got a big family and for years, we've had 5 rokus in out home. Usually at least 3 of them were playing simultaneously, Often combination with additional internet usage (online gaming, browsing etc.) We almost never had issues with our Rokus freezing or buffering. I can probably count on two hands the amount of times we had those issues over the course of 5-7 years. However, with the fire TV (especially when connected to freetime), we are constantly (at least once daily) having to hard reset our devices (all of them) because the software has frozen and is unusable. We've tried shutting down/turning off all of the options that use bandwidth in the background -there is an awful lot of it with this device- but to no avail. As far as speed goes and stability goes... roku is BY FAR (FAR FAR) the winner.2. The dashboard is a hot mess and is missing key features.I will explain more about why this is so problematic in the parental controls info below but basically, you are inside of amazon prime video when you turn on your tv. You do have the option to get inside of other apps (like netflix, hulu, youtube, etc.) but there is no organization. Apps are mixed in with games, videos, commercials etc. And on top of that, you have both free and paid content mixed onto the main page without any clear indicators of what's what. You have to click through the individual shows/movies to find out if it's included in your prime plan (or another paid subscription) or if it's an additional cost.Also - the screen saver options are pictures or ....pictures. Yep, that's all. Personally, I like to have a clock on my screensaver and although I can get a clock app (at an additional cost) for the device, it does not automatically display on the screen. You have to manually open the app to view the clock. For me, this sort of defeats the purpose.3. The fire tv remote app is a joke.If you don't have an Echo or Alexa device or you are using the parental controls on your firestick (see below) then be careful NOT to lose your remote. The app is difficult to use and only connects about half the time. EACH time you connect the remote to the tv, you have to set it up using an on-screen code. My kids lose their remotes DAILY and since we can not use Alexa on their devices... we often end up hunting for remotes or fighting with the app for 30 minutes. Perhaps I'm spoiled by the fair superior roku remote app but trust me... the firestick remote app is minimally functional at best.4. Alexa integration & Echo Dot DOES NOT WORK WITH PARENTAL CONTROLS ENABLED.For our family, this was a deal-breaker for sure. If you are purchasing this for the voice features to help your child with navigating, then be prepared. It does not work inside of the Freetime App or when parental controls are enabled. Which brings me to my next point5. Parental controls are just a bunch of fancy bells and whistles that do not do an adequate job.As far as parental controls on this device, there are basically 2 options and I will explain each of them and why neither one does the job.FREETIME APP - Freetime COULD be a really really great solution for parents everywhere. It's a fantastic start but needs some major improvements. The good thing about this app is that is that kids can only access things the parent allows and they can not exit the freetime app without a parental code. Even if they shut off the tv, it will start up inside of the freetime app. This is honestly pretty awesome.The problems - Of course, I already mentioned the issues with the freezing. I believe this could be fixed if Amazon created firesticks that were devoted to freetime only and did not have the amazon prime video app running in the background. Because you are working inside of an app, I think it creates issue.The second (huge) issue is that the kids can not control it by voice. This was the WHOLE POINT in our purchase. It doesn't look like amazon has any plans to correct this issue.The other option is to just allow your children to use the adult dashboard with the parental controls enabled. But this presents several big problems as well. For starters, the dashboard is an unorganized chaotic mess. Apps, games, tv shows (paid and free) are all mixed into the main screen along with advertisements and commercials (which can, fortunately, be set NOT to autoplay). There is no option to customize what shows up on your "dashboard" so basically, whatever Amazon chooses to display will be seen by your child. Some of the thumbnails for these videos are inappropriate and even scary for children at young ages. So trust me, you do not want your child wandering around in the dashboard even if they can't access the content. Also, if you are offended by R-rated and unrated content, you also may not enjoy the experience of wandering through your dash.Secondly, children can't access ANY content with parental controls on unless you enter a code each time (for every single episode.) So if your child wants to watch mickey mouse, you'll have to enter a code to access it from the adult dashboard (in the Freetime app, you can set allowed apps by age/rating and also individually). When the first mickey mouse episode ends, you'll have to enter the password AGAIN to watch the next episode. (Overkill much Amazon?) Also, as I mentioned above, there is no clear indicator of paid vs free content and since the content is all mixed together, kids get frustrated when they see something they want to watch but can not.AMAZON IF YOU'RE READING THIS: I think you could fix NEARLY everything on this list by simply allowing your customers to customize their main dashboards, I prefer my content sorted and I don't want inappropriate thumbnails on my TV screen when my children come into my room. Also, I need the ability on the adult dashboard to use parental controls to block ONLY specific content by age/rating and paid/free. I don't need the fancy Freetime app but if you insist, then please study how NetFlix has created their kid's dashboard and also add Alexa integration.

title: Slow navigation very frustrating
body: This is the slowest device I've used since cable internet became a thing. Sometimes itll take 30 seconds for an app to load and have clicks register. And once the app is loaded, the videos that are streamed take a long time to begin playing. Sometimes they never play and I have to reboot the device entirely by unplugging it, and then the apps work.The device cannot multitask. For instance, when you want to switch apps you essentially close one and open another new. This is a bigger deal than it sounds like since the device is so slow. If you think you can watch a basketball game then watch something else during the commercials like you do with cable, you're wrong. It'll take you 60 seconds to get something else playing, then take you 60 seconds to navigate back to the game. In an Xbox One, since several apps can be open at once, those are 10 second tasks or less. This is something nobody has managed to beat cable on yet.The video buffers constantly despite the fact that I have a very fast connection and no other device I use has this issue.I do not like the way Amazon really insists its own programming on you first. The home screen should be the app selection screen, not title selection. Instead, it takes several clicks and a scroll all the way to the right to get the screen where you can see all of your apps. Apple TV got this right, not sure why Amazon can't. Many would consider this a benefit however, it's an attempt to make the interface more seamless. A lot of people want to search for content without going through an additional layer of knowing what apps to load for that content. This makes sense in paper, but this product's execution is poor. And I wish it had the Apple TV's home screen.If I do not use an app for two weeks or so, it makes me reactivate it based in my cable subscription, which is annoying.It is annoying that it cannot be set up with a typical universal remote. It's now the only device I have that needs its own remote. Even my Xbox One can be controlled by the universal remote.The voice recognition is good, but Xfinity's is better.The number of apps is great, every network has an app and the global search across all apps is decent. However many networks have several apps. I don't know why CBS needs a sports app, a news app, and a regular programming app. And to watch the programming you need to sign into all 3 individually. Can't this be one app that just categorizes content? The Fire Stick should force all apps that require cable subscriptions to abide by the same API, and your cable subscription should be stored globally on the device, so when a new app is installed, that subscription is already set up and you just select it from the list for that app. I think somehow it auto loaded for me for one or two apps, but it doesn't anymore.The apps have a lot of commercials, but it's cool that they all have timers, so you know when the show is coming back on. Also, you'll see the same commercial over and over again with little variation. So there is really no reason to watch them. I was incredibly annoyed when I bought a movie on Amazon Prime and even so, there were commercials. What?The small size makes this very portable, you can bring it on trips with you. This is probably what it does best. If you travel a lot for work, this is very handy.The biggest benefits are it's portability and the fact that I only paid $15 (Prime Day), which is unbeatable. But it is the worst overall streaming device I've ever used. I use my Xbox One instead, and only use this device when staying in hotels. And if the wifi in the hotel is slow, I use 4G and watch on my laptop, so this does not get much use anymore.Edit: after a few months I decided to five up entirely on this. It's too slow, too many ads, it freezes, it's more frustration than it's worth.

title: UPDATE 6/2/2020
body: Update: 6/5/20When it works is awesome, easy to use and smooth.At first Firestick was awesome, had it for several months then it began freezing and I’d have to unplug it to get it to work again. Over and over. I finally called Amazon several times about the problem. The last call I had with them over this they helped me troubleshoot by restoring to factory settings then told me: If you keep having problems again we will go ahead and replace it. Not a couple of days later it froze again, it also was overheating out in the open , not locked up where it can’t breath.So I thought: I’ll give it one more week (maybe this time it froze, it was a glitch) then I’ll call back. It froze again. So I called again (this is the 4-5th call I’ve had with them over this problem by the way), the gal I spoke with wanted me to factory reset it again, pull out my heavy furniture to unplug my modem and router - my back is all messed up so I told her I could not do that and my other devices were were working just fine with the internet- it’s not the internet. She insisted we factor reset it again- I told her we already did that and that the last guy I spoke with said if it happened again - they would replace it - she did not care. I had to reset it again. ( I’m thinking in my head: this isn’t a thousand dollar device- what did it cost to make? $5.00 maybe?). So anyhow, we factory reset it , was on the phone with this gal for over a half hour. After all that she puts me on hold comes back on the phone and says: I spoke to my supervisor about the problem, he reluctantly agreed to replace it. I’m thinking why did you have me go though all that just to replace it?Anyhow, a week later I get my replacement, it’s a used fire stick that had been refurbished that was just thrown in a bag and sent to me. I couldn’t believe it. I didn’t purchase a used fire stick, I expected a new replacement. So I called again: Amazon said they would just refund me a portion of the money back and I return both Fire sticks: I said I’ve been a customer since amazon began and have purchased who knows how many items with the , I have 2 amazon credit cards I’ve never been late paying them off. I couldn’t believe this treatment over a stupid fire stick.She put me on hold and decided to give me the full refund.I asked why they sent me a used refurbished? She said they didn’t have any new ones to send out. I looked on the website and they are selling them - they are not back ordered. 🤷‍♀️Anyhow, I’m returning both and gonna try the 4K. If I didn’t already have the fire recast to go with the fire stick I’d go elsewhere to buy a different streaming device after all this mess.So I’m not sure if my fire stick was the only one that freezes and heats up but either way I hope this review helps you make your decision on weather to purchase this item or not.1st initial review;I love the Firestick. It’s easy to use, handy and has a great interface.The only downside is the amount of storage. It’s just not enough.I would still recommend it if you are only going to be using a few apps on it. Ie; Amazon Video, Netflix, Apple TV , etc,. Space fills up fast.

title: My Fire Stick failed in less than six months. SMH
body: First the update: It was easy to set up, but apart from that my Fire Stick has been a dodgy, bug ridden device from very early on, prone to slow boot-ups and losing connection at times. But it has always, until today, settled down and allowed me to connect to what I was wanting to watch. Today, not even six months from date of purchase and installation, it totally stopped being able to keep connected to the router for more than a few seconds at time. Today it has been on a repeating loop of dropping the connection, booting up, taking me briefly to its home page and then losing connection again as soon as I try to navigate into the home page. Four times it tried to run through a software update--one that took 10 minutes to complete each time.After doing everything I could think of to troubleshoot the device (and I mean EVERYTHING), I called Customer Service for help. The two persistent CS agents who worked with me did everything they could to solve the problem. In fact, we must have repeated the process six or seven times in the more than 90 minute-long troubleshooting session. Finally after exhausting every possible option multiple times, someone somewhere along the line relented and issued me a voucher to use to replace the device at no cost to me. I'm not sure why this process had to be so hard. Well, I do understand, actually. Turns out the warranty on a Fire TV Stick is just 90 days. Mine failed at just under six months--which gives you an idea of how little confidence Amazon has in its devices when you think about it.So, here's my advice if you're thinking about buying a Fire TV Stick. Be aware of how short the warranty is--only 90 days. The fact that they were finally willing to replace it for me at no further cost to me is really a testimony to my own perseverance on the phone today, since essentially I bore with the Customer Service's many attempts to fix the problem until they wore themselves out and gave up. Made in China may be the cheap way to go, but does it lead to quality and good performance? Not really.My original review: The set up was easy, and it took just a few minutes to learn how to navigate the device. So far, I'm satisfied with my purchase. The one thing I really wanted to comment on, in case anyone is interested, is that while I can plug the Fire Stick directly into my Smart TV as the instructions say, doing that prevents me from pairing it with the Bose sound system. The good news is that my Bose console has both an HMDI port as well as a USB connection right in the front control panel. I found that by connecting the Fire Stick to the Bose console via those two ports I can watch with the Fire Stick and enjoy that wonderful surround sound of my Bose system at the same time. In the setup there was the advisory that the USP port was underpowered, but that doesn't seem to make a difference in the streaming. There is a USB port on the front of my Verizon Set Top Box, too, and that port also works with the Fire Stick as a power source. I have the option of plugging it into a wall outlet, but not without quite a bit of inconvenience, so for now I'll leave it as is.

title: Trash. Absolute Trash. Get a Roku
body: This product is absolutely horrid. I mean it streams, but that's about it. Sometimes it doesn't even do that without freezing and glitching. The user interface is garbage and is, like all other Amazon products, extremely centered around generating maximum ad revenue. To top it off, the home screen constantly bombards you with those ads and are constantly encouraging you to spend more money through Amazon. We get it. You want more of our money. We don't have to be reminded that we're all economic slaves to corporate greed and monopolies when we're just trying to get to an app and watch a movie.The greed and control coming from these mega corporations is absolutely ridiculous. You can't watch a 30 second video FB without having to sit through their ad. You can't watch a video on youtube without their ads. And with Amazon, you can't even watch a movie on apps or switch through apps on the home screen without first having to navigate through the heavy home screen advertisements that take up most of the screen. This is extremely frustrating for my elders who have bought a fire sticks and aren't the most tech savvy. They often end up accidentally clicking on the ads, get lost, and have to start over. I have 11 aunts and uncles. I've switched or introduced at least 7 of them to Roku. Most liked them so much that they now have several Rokus for multiple rooms.They bought a Roku and their tech problems were solved. The Roku navigation is cleaner, simpler, the remote responds better (granted it does feel a little cheaper), Roku is constantly improving their voice command feature, their advertisements are only a small section of the home screen, the amount of apps and streaming platforms that you can download on Roku is massive since it isn't hindered by Amazon's need to nickle and dime everyone from customer to seller on everything. Most of all, I've never had a Roku glitch on me. The fire stick streams, and that's why I gave it two stars instead of one. Other than that, it's terrible and I've had nothing but hassle with it, for myself and my elder relatives. Save yourself the money, the headache, and the aggravation. I would suggest getting yourself a Roku streaming device instead.PS. The Roku remotes aren't as much as just buying a new device like the Fire Stick remotes are.

title: Audio dropouts make this unusable for me
body: My wife purchased this on Prime day. for a very good price.My system: 40 inch Toshiba HDMI tv from 2010, Bose surround sound with JVC receiver, 100 mpbs internet connection. Sound goes from TV to the receiver via Optical cable. My current streaming device is connected via Ethernet and also has its own optical cable to the receiver and does Dolby digital.Plus: The fire stick identified my TV correctly and allowed me to pick the brand of receiver. The remote controls the sound on the receiver. The switch seems to turn off all devices. Alexa integration worked fine and was setup correctly without going into the Alexa app. Some of the apps are better than the apps on my original streaming device, for example Philo TV has a couple of days you can scroll to on the Guide, where my other Streamer only has 7 hours at best. It has a browser; I thought this was cute kind of like WebTV in the old days.Minus: The Home display is very busy; many of my apps appear on multiple places on the home screen. The most recent list seems to add everything to the front. The biggest issue is the LIVE TV band. Why you would waste processor time on this is beyond me. If I want to watch live TV I’ll go into my app for live TV. Pass thru audio seems to be sending Stereo instead of surround sound. I could live with Stereo for my second device. The only thing that does surround sound is Prime and Netflix anyway.But Audio drops outs are the big issue for me. This seems to do pass thru from the HDMI cable into the TV and then down to the receiver via the Optical cable, but every minute or so the sound cuts out. I have tried changing the audio on the fire stick to Stereo, tried different HDMI ports, and tried different setting on the TV as well with no success. I can’t even get it to play a whole song from Prime music without the sound cutting out. This makes it unusable for me. Guess I will be sticking to my other streaming device. I have four of the other brands devices from the basic stick to the Ultra and have no issues with sound via the pass thru. So if you have an older TV with toslink cables/ coax cable for 5.1 surround sound I’d skip this one.

title: Has trouble connecting brand new and weeks later
body: I went with this firestick as i wanted to try something different from the Roku streaming devices. I do enjoy the interface but there are some things about the firestick that really bother me. First it took forever for the device to sync with the Amazon servers - saying there is an issue with the ISP. I work for an ISP so to place blame on the internet provider is not cool (had this issue with Amazon Echo also). Just to paint the picture my access point for this fire stick is literally like 3 ft away from this and won't connect...yet my laptops, phones and other devices have no issues accessing the internet? I tried from a different access point and still no luck (both 5g and 2.4g networks) so the initial setup was not the most seamless. I was surprised to find out I have the same issue a few weeks later when it decided to disconnect on it's on.I use this mainly to stream music only, no video but a device like this suits my needs. As I have several AP's around my house to cover my area with Ethernet capabilities at all locations. Of course Ethernet is not incorporated into this device (you can purchase one but i did not based on the reviews). I would have like to see at least a hard line connection included with this but I am not going to spend extra money for something I use about once per week.The other flaw that I have discovered in the interface is the Alexa integration with premium services (Spotify) when i first linked my account it had no problem opening the application and launching was was commanded...I thought this was really neat at first until I literally tried again and there was a message more or less - Be sure your services are linked with your amazon/alexa account to enable this feature. Of course when I went in to verify if the accounts are linked they were there and set up properly. This completely debunks anything I would use voice commands for.Take it with a grain of salt as i do honestly believe for the price point of the device, it is a great deal and product. Just be prepared to "Call your ISP" when the only device not working in my entire home are amazon devices

title: Maybe I am the only person who prefers the previous generation firestick.
body: I am probably one of the few people who prefers the previous generation firestickI have multiple rooms in my house, each has a television and firestick in each room. My first two devices are the previous generation sans alexa voice control. My second set are new generation units. I continually have the two new units prompting me to allow alexa to control all of my devices. I AM NOT INTERESTED IN THIS. I DO NOT WANT THIS. I WILL NOT DO THIS. This is why I do not own anything with alexa or siri implanted in it. If there is anyway to disable Alexa on the new generation units please post how to do so. I own these for how they can be customized. Since my original purchase I have come to love the firestick. I was able to cut the cord as many people are doing today saving TONS on cable bills.The most frustrating part both the new units had was during set up, the volume control function. BOTH of my new units buffered for extended periods of time. The first of the new units(firestick number 3) took almost an hour to sync then progressed and allowed me to finish set up. The second(firestick number 4) continued to buffer for hours. I actually left it on low volume thru the nite and when I woke up it was still buffering and playing the "elevator music" as it tries to sync to allow the remote to control the volume. I do not know if this is an individual defect but having both devices I purchased experience problems really bothers me. Also the multiple steps needed to register the new devices before completion really are annoying. My original devices did not require the multiple steps.Once I was able to get past the delays, and able to configure my devices the way I prefer I have found they are more or less operating as they should. My other pet peeve is just like my mobile device, certain apps are not able to be removed. They are taking up more room on the device that could otherwise be used for content I want.I personally hope Amazon brings back the previous generation firesticks. If they do I will DEFINITELY BUY THEM.Once I was able to complete set up and

title: Support is not all that spectacular. Service is lacking. Price is attractive.
body: Update: this device is okay for streaming Netflix. It does not work well with others to including Amazon Prime Videos (regularly choppy, regularly loses continuity between audio and video). I have 3 of these in my home two located within a few feet of Wi-Fi with +500Mbps, and even they drop off or lose connectivity with the network frequently. I would strongly recommend spending you money on a device that functions well as a streaming device. This just isn’t it. Terrible rating for everything except set-up.Bought one during Amazon Day for a low cost option to ease the streaming use of my gaming console. Arrived in a box carefully guarded by many pillows of air within a larger box. Plugged it in, got to working right away. Downloaded apps to stream with, signing into the various apps with each credential set as required. Had some fuzzy streams here and there, no biggie. Moved it a lesser utilized 5G Wi-Fi and fuzzy streams are less common. Moved it from the bedroom to the living room TVs a few times, no problems.Purchased a second stick at regular retail price a few months later. This time it was shipped in a poorly sealed bubble envelope. The package arrived without any sign of merchandise. After nearly two weeks of waiting for my account to be credited, I ordered another stick. This arrived in the same sort of packaging as the first. I plugged it up and hooked it in, got to downloading, repeating all of the steps of the first one. All seems to go smoothly. Remained up touched from Wednesday until Saturday evening. Now it flashes a sort of start screen consisting of the Amazon logo then dies. Called support and was asked to remove the extender and try different HDMI ports. Nothing of the troubleshooting solved the issue. Support said they will email a return shipping label for me to print off and attach to a packaging for the return of the fire stick. It will be tested and evaluated by their technicians. After their evaluation, they will return that stick or a new one. End of story for now.

title: Doesn't work with Disney Plus - get the 4K version or a Roku instead to use Disney Plus
body: Netflix and Prime run great so it those are your primary services and don't plan on ever using Disney you'll be fine. But be warned Disney Plus can't run on this version of the Firestick. After installing the app and signing in, the app progress spinner just spins and spins and then the app times out with an network connection error. We tried a number of fixes like resetting the router, re-installing the app, resetting the Firestick etc. Let's face it, a streaming device built in 2020 shouldn't require those levels of work around just to get super popular app to run on the device in the first place. While this could be a problem with the Disney app, Amazon testing should have caught this and simply blocked D+ as an eligible app on this version or worked with Disney to get it fixed. We also have a 4K Firestick and Disney Plus runs like a dream on it. I deeply regret not buying another of those devices rather than this one and really glad we got this on sale as we didn't waste too much money on it. What's a bit more alarming is that there are reviews where folks report success running Disney Plus on this device which made us think we'd be okay. Perhaps this version of the Firestick has inconsistent network internals - so maybe you'll get lucky if you buy a "good" one, but you are rolling the dice. Also frustrating is that we have a 4 year old Roku that runs Disney like champ, so no idea what's going on with this Firestick. Bottom line, if you want Disney go with the 4K version or Roku. Another suggestion I've heard for a fix is using hard wired Ethernet and the adapter, but seriously if a Firestick sitting in the same room with our wireless router that happily delivers over 100mps to every other wireless device in the room can't use that bandwidth to make Disney Plus work something is very wrong with the device. For example I can hold my IPad up next to the Firestick and Disney Plus starts right up and streams wonderfully. In fact it even runs fine on our 8 inch Fire tablet. Sigh.

title: Wifi Issues are not your internet
body: It is unbelievable that no one will admit that this device has connectivity issues. No one has been able to offer a solution for those whose internet is fine. Replies by "experts" and those who claim to know how to troubleshoot this problem seem to offer the same stock answers and ignore what users are ACTUALLY saying about their individual experiences. If anyone trying to troubleshoot the issuewho claims to have any sense at all, they would STOP telling people to check their internet connection. 90 percent of the complaints claim their internet connection is fine because ALL their other devices have NO issues. How hard is that to understand. IT IS THE FIRESTICK that has connectivity issues. If another person tells some poor soul to check their internet connwction,I swear...I dare someone to actually give a real solution (BAR DOING A HARDWIRE) to this problem besides telling people to disable features, restarting the firestick or modem or any other solution that totally ignores the fact that most people with this issue have NO, I repeat, for those who are hard of comprehending, NO issues ALL other devices. And in case someone was wondering, I did try putting it closer to the router and also using HDMI extension cable. Oh yes. Checked my speed, too. It jumps up and down dramatically causing the buffering issue. AGAIN, for those hard of comprehension, not my internet because all other devices maintain a good connection. Again, If someone has a real solution to the Wi-Fi issue...UPDATEI had to order the adapter tyo hardwire it. Now it streams flawlessly. The Wi-Fi, on this device only, fluctuated in speed so bad it will never be adequate. Bottom line- if your internet connection is not 100mbps or higher, in my estimation, your Wi-Fi will connect at half that or less and then add in the throttling (fluctuating the speed) and you will get buffering. Sad that it took me hours of reading reviews and scouring the internet get the whole picture-no pun intended.

title: Disappointed with app selection and streaming sports
body: After thinking about purchasing a Fire Stick for a while, I was given one as a gift. I can safely say that I'm happy I didn't spend my own money on one. It may be a lot better with Alexa, but I don't have Alexa.The first thing I did was add the NHL app. The Fire Stick had issues with streaming the games. They were relatively clear but the Fire Stick is unable to maintain the resolution during game action. The problem with that is obvious. By comparison ROKU and cable TV are both able to maintain clarity throughout the action.I checked out some movies and some Amazon shows to see how they faired. They looked great, but since my cable TV has the Amazon Prime Video app, it makes the Fire Stick unnecessary.What I needed a streaming device for was to run apps that aren't available through my cable. I wanted to try Fire Stick because I have another nearby TV on ROKU. Another ROKU would only cause cross signal issues with the remotes. Unfortunately, Fire Stick doesn't allow some of these apps. Most notably VUDU and AMC Theaters On Demand. Amazon seems to think it's fine because I can access the same content through other apps, but in the case of something like AMC Theaters there's the issue of receiving credit towards rewards for AMC Stubs Premiere members such as myself.I also wanted to add the Xfinity beta app because I don't use a cable converter for my other TV. So I'm using my ROKU to stream my cable instead. If Fire Stick would run this app I could switch it to the other TV and use the ROKU on my main TV to stream the other apps Fire Stick won't run.So, as long as Amazon seems to feel it's okay to ask customers to utilize alternatives, I'll continue using ROKU as an alternative to Fire Stick.Fire Stick may deserve more stars. It's a fine streaming device. Maybe it has advantages I'm missing? I don't know. As it is now, for me personally it's a bit of a bust. So I'm giving it 2 stars.

title: It’s great when it works properly and doesn’t lag
body: I purchased this as an upgrade from an older Apple TV and it has made my media experience much more convenient. It is relatively easy to set up to your TV and I like how I can control the power button with one remote. Being able to control the volume is also a great feature, but the buttons to control the volume are not as soft. When the volume fluctuates a lot in a show, I find that I grab my original TV remote to quickly lower the volume.Another issue I’ve had is that when I overuse Alexa, some of the apps don’t function properly. It can be inconsistent sometimes, and I find that apps start working again after you restart the firestick once or twice. The fact that I can connect my cable account and download my favorite channel is huge plus. However, watching Live TV tends to lag sometimes.UPDATE: The automatic sign-in has ruined my experience with this device. I used to be able to watch cable networks by using my TV provider, but ever since I changed my Spectrum password I cannot access programs on cable apps, such as HGTV, MTV, USA or Bravo.I have tried absolutely everything to fix this issue. I signed out of these TV provider apps and tried signing back in but every time I try to do so it just signs me in automatically without giving me the chance to manually type my username or password. I have rebooted my device to the default factory settings. I have deleted these TV provider apps and tried to download it again but it always automatically signs me in. I have even called Amazon Customer Service to ask for help, and when I tried explaining the issue I LITERALLY GOT YELLED AT! When I asked to speak to her supervisor because she had a tone with me, she just redirected me without apologizing for her rude behavior. I have tried everything and I’m not sure what to do because Amazon won’t even help me.

title: Disappointing
body: We bought our first Fire Stick back on October 8, 2019. It worked so well we cancelled our 20 year relationship with Direct TV. We liked it so much we bought a second one for downstairs within a week. The first one sometimes will lock up and we have to reboot to fix it. I can live with that. We were still very happy with the Fire Sticks and bought another one last week for our daughter's TV. Then a few days ago the second Fire Stick started displaying a warning that the memory was getting full when it was turned on. It suggested deleting one or more APPs to restore some memory. I did that, but when I checked the memory there was no change. Then the warning started popping up while browsing and then while watching a show. This got so irritating that I ended up uninstalling every APP that wasn't factory installed. I rebooted the Fire Stick and still no change in the memory. One of the options on the manage storage screen was to do a Factory Reset that would erase all content and storage on the device except the factory APPs, make it like it came out of the box. OK so I did that. When it started up it asked what language, OK English; then it found my internet connection and I signed on, connection success, so far so good; then Finding Updates, "Unable to update your Fire TV Stick" Try disconnecting the power for a few seconds then plug it back in. Did that, didn't work. "If that does not work contact us at www.amazon.com/devicesupport"Followed the URL to the site and there was no support there, just support sales merchandise: support books, support shelves, support hose, support socks, etc. I had to spend about 20 minutes digging deep into the site to finally get to a Fire Stick device help page but none of the trouble selections came close to fitting my issue.

title: Loved it, remote stopped working after a month.
body: I usually don’t leave reviews like this, I really want to like stuff and give people a chance but I have to share my experience.Initially we really loved the product, as far as streaming it worked great, we’ve yet to really use the Alexa feature but that’s okay too. The reason for my star rating is after a month our remote stopped working, just completely unresponsive so at first we thought it was batteries and we changed them, then we had to unplug the whole system, change hdmi ports the whole deal.After two weeks of this happening completely randomly (I tried the system on other TVs, ports, with and without hdmi dongle etc), I finally called customer support, they walked me though resetting the remote which worked and then not even an hour later same thing happened completely unresponsive and a black screen (tv still turned on and off just fine), so I called them back, I was on the phone for almost 40 minutes unplugging and replugging the whole system all over the place, opening and closing apps and making them each play a movie for a few minutes to “stress test” the stick, then I was told they would call me back after two hours and to leave a movie playing, they called back same issue so we reset everything and updated, I was told that it was simply because I hadn’t updated that I have this issue and that “it only takes a couple minutes to check so I should do it more” now it’s been two days (no new update) and once again the screen froze and the remote is completely unresponsive I don’t know if it’s just defective or if other people have had this issue.At this point Im so frustrated with the stick and the customer service that I absolutely would not recommend this product, you’re better off without a smart tv than with this.

title: Seriously Amazon...
body: Very disappointed with this piece of junk... First of all, you're suppose to get 8G with the stick to download the apps you want, however Amazon takes up 85% of that space with useless garbage they want you to have and there's no way to uninstall it (such as: GameCircle, Amazon Pictures, Amazon Music, Amazon Family Time, etc.) Some people may want that stuff but it should be at the user's choice not forced upon everyone. If this is how Amazon is going to operate then they should re-issue new FireTV Stick's to those who own one with larger storage capacity like 12G or 24G. Second, buffering is horrible. You can't watch anything without it jamming up and stalling, the picture quality if far worse than a pirated copy video/disc as it's out of focus (blurry) most of the time. Called tech support about all these issues and all I got was "Well that's the way it's set up. Nothing we can do about it." Amazon and Jeff Bezos had better put the money down they're counting from ripping off subscribers and customers and start paying more attention to their products they're pandering onto the public. Amazon use to be a good, decent company...not anymore. They raise their prices on everything especially their Prime Membership -- which is ridiculous and price gouging -- and they expect people to just fork over their money. They remove their APP from all TVs so you can't have quality viewing anymore and expect you to buy a cheap piece of junk like their FireTV Stick. I'm going to get rid of mine and go to ROKU or some other product. This is all a setup for Amazon and Bezos to "force" their next product on the public -- Amazon TVs. Third, as one other reviewer mentioned it is ridiculous what you have to go through to replace the batteries on the cheap remote.

title: Device is a bit slow to respond to commands.
body: This streaming device works descent once it is streaming. The drawback to it can be compared to a slow computer. I use Playstation Vue , CBS All Access , Netflix , and Sirius XM Internet Radio.When you start to navigate apps, it seems to move smoothly most of the time. When I actually load the app and move around looking for what to stream in CBS and Vue it can be very slow to respond. (You select a program and it sits there for 5-10 seconds before going.) So you don't know if it took the command or not because like computers it does not tell you that it is thinking. When you ask Alexa to change channels it sometimes has the blue bar at the top of the screen come up immediately, other times it takes 5-10 seconds to respond that it is listening. I feel the processor in this unit is too slow for my tastes.Also using normal FF/REW on Vue I noticed sometimes would close the app and go to home screen. However you can use the circle to FF/REW and it will not stream the FF/REW, it goes ahead in 10 sec. increments which works descent similar to Netflix FF/REWOther than that I think it is a good unit. If you can stand the lag you get when sending commands and are patient and you want a budget unit that at least streams well. I do not have any connection issues through my WiFi set up using 5G. So the unit seems to pick up well. (My WiFi Modem is upstairs and the Fire Stick is downstairs.For a little more money the 4K Fire Stick works awesome for me. I bought both so I could use 2 tv's. This stick is going back and I've ordered the 4K version which I like better for the speed of the navigation of menus.Keep in mind the 4K version will work with regular HD tv's as well.( With HDMI input. )

title: replacement also starts failing after 2 days
body: Got a replacement for the first firestick showing repeatable failure to stream uninterrupted. The next one worked initially without showing the same symptoms but this lasted only for 2 days. Now it starts with the same behavior of rebuffering and at times restarting a session from the beginning. The firestick fails miserable in comparison to my roku 3. Streaming simultaneously the same internet content on my notebook leaves no question to who is at fault. The firestick design, HW + FW + SW is just not ready for prime time. I am giving it a last try on netflix streaming but suggest to consider alternative options if possible.Update 5/15/2019Quick follow-up on my findings with the replacement fireTV stick which I just returned for refund1: It fails on our Netflix movie rather badly with going into buffering intermittently to force us going to our roku 3 to at least continue the showing uninterruptedAs an alternative I purchased the fireTV 4K stick and did a side-by-side comparison of both versions giving me some good news about the 4K stick running on the same video streamed over IN uninterrupted where as the base version starting to reset the session from the beginning after initial attempt to rebuffer.For now I regard the fireTV 4K superior in many ways but noticed a general problem with the buffering algorithm. At the start of the video the buffering is inadequate so the video starts in low resolution and only over ~10-20sec it catches up to deliver full resolution. This seems to be the common problem but the base stick seems not to be able to cope with such situation smoothly.In conclusion: try the fireTV 4K if you experience similar issues with the base version

title: It's great when it works but other times it's practically unusable
body: Where to begin? I guess the cons first- Remote control lags ALL the time. Some apps like plex, where the toolbar disappears after like 5 seconds if you want to pause, change streaming settings, add subtitles suffer from this because the lag sometimes won't even register until seconds later. This happens with the app AND the physical remote. How do you somehow mess up a remote this badly?- It constantly drops connections or the connection is very slow often and nothing helps (again, taking plex as an example, this app suffers because it requires a good connection otherwise it drops streams or I have to watch is sub-480 quality, which isn't great unless I'm putting something like, idk, King of the Hill or The Simpsons where I've seen every season like a million times and putting it on as I fall asleep or doing chores- Going back to the remote app, how do you also screw up so much to the point where you don't even have pause/play/rewind/forward buttons on there, just a back, home, and the 'hamburger menu' button. The touch controls are hard to get used to and there should really be a fake up/down/left/right button for people who need a traditional button layout. I get they were trying to be like apple tv, but those controls are trash too.- Alexa integration to any app sucks. The only thing it even works well for is for the amazon stuff, and even then it doesn't even work that greatThe literal only pro is sometimes it works really well but that's only like 20% of the time.I bought this to replace a broken roku and I regret not just getting a roku because this thing is garbage. Thank god I bought it on sale otherwise I would have been really upset

title: Good value but terrible Bluetooth audio support
body: I purchased the fire TV stick for my bedroom projector system. The fire stick is plugged into the projector which is mounted on the ceiling. I bought the fire stick because it claims to support Bluetooth audio to connect to wireless speakers or headphones. This is important since I don’t want to run audio cables from the ceiling all the way over to the speakers, which are on the other side of the room. Setting up the Fire stick and pairing to my speakers was easy enough, but I was dismayed to find that the volume buttons on the remote DO NOT WORK to control the loudness for Bluetooth devices. The speakers have their own volume control buttons, so I figured I could just set the volume manually on the speakers and hope that I wouldn’t need to frequently adjust it. I was again met with a rude awakening that the volume is wayyyy too loud even with my speaker volume set to the minimum possible. Unwatchably loud. And there is no way to fix it. I did some googling and found that this has been an issue known by Amazon since 2017!!Amazon, please prioritize this bug. Poor Bluetooth audio support is not acceptable in 2019. Most audio devices are Bluetooth these days. I have an LG smart projector in another room and it handles BT volume control just fine using that remote, on the very same speakers. This is clearly a bug with your implementation of Bluetooth audio controls.So my two hours of messing around with this thing have been wasted. Had to go to target and buy a Chromecast. Will be returning the fire tv stick, please fix your Bluetooth audio support and I’ll consider trying again in the future.

title: Not the greatest, but not the worst
body: I'm very disappointed in this thing. First of all, I was forced to buy it because Amazon Prime no longer likes Sony Blu-Ray. I don't know why. The discount because of the forced purchase was nice, but still--it's a forced purchase. I followed the directions for the first FireStick, which involved me entering my very long email address a number of times, along with passwords, one... character... at... a... time. using arrow keys. Took a long time! Then, the thing only worked for a short time. The next day (Friday), we were met with a green screen. I spent my Friday evening online, chatting with someone, who had me reinstall the thing....with my very long....information....and arrows. Ultimately, all the shows were showing as IMDb and not Prime. Then, the customer service guy determined it was a faulty FireStick and I must return it. So, enter finding packaging, going to UPS, etc. The replacement came quickly and not in the 5-10 business days I was told, so that was nice. Then, I spent my evening with the new FireStick entering lots of characters with arrows to enter everything again. However, the shows displayed as Prime for only one night. The next night, everything was showing as IMDb, which gives a commercial before the show. Once again, I spent the evening chatting with Customer Service to no avail. Resetting, re-entering until 9:00 p.m. when I gave up. We are able to watch the shows, but I don't understand this IMDb thing, and I don't want to spend an entire evening with Customer Service trying to figure it out. The very first time of use, it was great for a few hours. Since then, not so great.

title: The Number of 5 Star Reviews Makes Me Distrust Amazon
body: You Might Like This If: You like a cool looking and easy to use interface.You Might Not Like This If: You expect streaming television apps actually stream content without freezing up and requiring multiple reboots.TL;DR - Honestly, I don't understand the high reviews. My fire stick is on a TV that is less than 3 feet from my router. The signal is strong to the smart TV, other smart TVs in the house, laptops, cellphones, and our security system. Yet the fire stick routinely tells me the signal is bad.The apps don't always work. HBO is especially bad, but I have problems with the Amazon videos, too. Also FX. That might be the fault of the developer...but if Amazon is selling a streaming device that's supposed to be compatible with the channels I want to watch, then the burden of making sure the apps work is on them.If I pause a movie I know it will take me two or three attempts to get it to play again. At a minimum. Sometimes it won't relaunch at all. Or the audio will start playing while the video is frozen.Does Amazon have support for that? Nah. They tell you to reboot. Or maybe delete the app and re-install. Because that's exactly what you want to do in the middle of a movie that you paused for a few minutes. It's like their IT department is someone's dad from the 90s telling you to just hit CTRL+Alt+DEL or slap the television to get better signal. Absurd.I finally gave up and switched to a competitor that has an inferior interface BUT ACTUALLY WORKS WHEN I PRESS PLAY 100% of the time. Like it should.

title: NOT for metered data networks, and a little glitchy
body: I bought two Fire sticks and a 1TB Fire Recast on sale from Best Buy. The idea was to use them at our cabin to get over the air television channels, and record them using the Recast DVR. We only have satellite Internet at the cabin which is metered and slows down after our data limit is met. I've found if left connected to the Internet, these devices will draw large amounts of data (doing what I don't know). However, Amazon doesn't appear to have designed these devices for people with metered data connections, but rather for city and suburban areas where data is cheap and unlimited. I try to keep the devices unplugged most of the time, however, if not connected to the Internet they will not interface well with the Recast DVR (and often will not play recorded programs). Some comments here indicate that the Recast will work with the Fire sticks on the same WiFi router even when not connected to the Internet. However, I have found this not entirely true -- rather they will often not play recorded programs without Internet connection, even when they are otherwise connected to the Recast by WiFi. Also, both Fire sticks randomly turn off sometimes after about 30 minutes use -- strange. So, these might be good for the city or if you have unlimited Internet data so that you can afford to keep them connected to the Internet at all times. However, they are frustrating for those who need them the most -- people who want to watch and record over the air channels because they don't have unlimited data plans to stream programming.

title: FIRESTICK RIP-OFF AND RESOLUTION
body: Amazon stopped supporting Prime video oncertain Sony Bravia smart TVs in September , 2019 ,forcing prime customers to purchase an expensive Amazon firestick if they wanted to continue to get Amazon prime that they already are paying for monthly. Although this is a "rip off", I decided that "it is cheaper to keep her" and instrad of buying a new Android TV, I bought the fire stick and sprung for the $40.00. The installation instructions were inadequate so I called "customer service"I was kept on the phone for almost two hours to discuss installing the the Amazon "fire stick" by a very kind and respectful woman who was unfamiliar with the product, was difficult to understand, and who provided mis-information regarding the continued availability of existing applications on my Sony TV without using the fire stick and insisted that I had to re-istall all of my applications.I was told that without installing the Amazon "fire stick" on my Sony "smart" TV , I woild be unableto contiue to access Amazon Prime videos and my other applications like Netflix etc. I asked to speak to a supervisor and was told none was available. I WAS SOLD A PRODUCT THAT EXCEEDED MY NEEDS and the time wasted already, and the time necessary for a return, exceeds the cost of the product. I was an unhappy customer.However, a Customer care person followed up with a phone call after I posted my original review and arranged for a satisfactory and fair resolution. Customer service to the rescue....

title: Remote Stopped Working. Amazon Does Virtually Nothing
body: I do like the Fire TV Stick. The Stick itself works fine. The problem I have is with the remote. It quit working after maybe three uses. I, of course, changed the batteries, went through all of the Help and Setup and Troubleshooting pages, reset the remote, and it just does not work anymore. I contacted Amazon Support through the chat feature and explained the problem. The representative said that since the warranty had expired, they could not send me a new remote, but I could BUY a new one, or use the app. I'm certainly not going to buy a new remote that costs probably pennies to produce, being a piece of lightweight plastic, and I did not like being told to just use the app when I paid for a remote that should last more than three uses.So if you want to use the remote rather than rely on your phone to control the Fire TV Stick, do not expect a lot of use out of the remote. There are lots of similar products out there. Maybe consider one of those, or at least check reviews carefully and see what people say about longevity. If your remote or stick quits working after the warranty period, Amazon will not be there for you.When it comes time to get a new or additional streaming device, I will not be getting another Fire TV Stick. Amazon Customer Service did nothing to help this customer, other than offering a 15% discount on a new remote. Very disappointing that they do not stand behind their products and do virtually nothing for their customers.

title: Disappointing
body: I bought a Fire TV Stick several years ago and had to return it because the remote was a piece of junk. Given the tenor of my conversation with Amazon customer service, I’m fairly confident the problem was widespread and that they were well aware of it.Well, not one to nurse old wounds, I decided to give the new “improved” model a try. I was interested in its compatibility with Echo speakers and the remote’s ability to control the TV’s power and volume. All of those functions seem to work pretty well — at least for now.HOWEVER, the device is constantly buffering. I did not have this problem with my old Roku. Yesterday, the Fire TV Stick began refusing to connect to YouTube. Out of the blue seemingly. I tried all the “fixes” suggested online to no avail. Retrieved my old Roku from the drawer and it connects to YouTube without a problem. I noticed online that others are experiencing the same problem. Not sure if the problem is with Google or Amazon. And I don’t care. Roku works great with Google’s YouTube.I’m keeping the Fire Stick because I bought it for half price and because it does connect to my Echo speaker for late night viewing/listening. Obviously I can’t watch YouTube but if there is something decent on Amazon, the Fire stick may be able to access it without buffering it’s brains out. We’ll see. Bottom line — don’t pay full price and have a working Roku as your primary streaming device.

title: Underpowered for new UI. Can't opt-out of firmware upgrade so it has become usuable.
body: I went all-in with the Firestick "ecosystem" in August of 2020. I bought this Firestick, Fire TV Recast, and a Toshiba FireTV. Everything was fantastic for the first 3 months. The Firestick was noticeably slower than the built-in FireTV but it was still usable for Netflix, Prime Video, Recast DVR, etc.As expected, Amazon would occasionally upgrade the firmware on all my devices. The most recent firmware upgrade with the UI change makes made my 1-year old firestick unusable with most notably: 1) long delays between the remote and the firestick, 2) long load times for apps, and 3) poor network performance (probably due to the high cpu loads). There is no way to go back to the older "more basic" UI. My more powerful FireTV handled the upgrade fine, albeit did get a bit more sluggish.Look, I understand technology quickly gets out-dated and I appreciate Amazon's effort to make a great product. But in this case, I didn't ask to upgrade to the new UI and I should have been given the option to remain with the old firmware. I feel like this rapid "planned obsolescence" is done simply to sell the newest version of a firestick.I don't care if you don't support your legacy products, but please don't make them useless with forced upgrades. I still have 20+ year old computers and 10+ year old phones that will technically work. Even Apple old Apple iphones still have some utility in them. My 1 year-old Firestick has none.

title: Frustrated With Repeated Failures
