This Amazon Fire TV Stick was super easy to setup and use, and very handy! The only negatives are as follows. At home my remote has a "guide" button, where I can scroll through the available channels to see what's on. The Fire remote doesn't have a guide button, you have to access it through the app, which is a bit cumbersome. Also, home Wifi seems to vary from a strong signal to a weak one. It's in constant flux, and this shows up on the TV. Sometimes the picture degrades from Hi Def to SD (standard definition), so that is a bummer. But that is not the fault of the Fire TV Stick.I love this product and recommend it. It works well with the Dish Anywhere App. It's easy to setup and use. It's inexpensive and well constructed.

title: Happy with the purchase as a Roku replacement
body: I purchased this during Prime Day, more from curiosity than need. I was using a gen 2 Roku in my master bedroom, hooked up to a 780p Visio TV. After setting this device up and using for a couple days, I won't be going back. It's a great little product. Let me tell you why....Setup: it's super easy to install. The device walks you through setup, and if your home is already "Alexa friendly" you won't even need to enter your wifi password.Remote: works well, and the voice function is nice. The best thing about this remote is it's ability to control my TV power and volume. It's like magic. During setup, it simply detected my television model and starting working. So, no more multiple remotes!Customization: this device works with youtube. The app isn't pre-installed, but if you do a quick search it will install the youtube app from the store. Also, you can turn on the developer settings very easily allowing you to basically install android services from anywhere. You can look up a "how to" video online.Use: This device is snappy and quick. and the response time is nice, particularly compared to my older device. It makes my TV feel 5 years newer. Adding my Vudu movie collection by using the MoviesAnywhere app was a breeze, so now my multiple video collections are all in one place. The search and voice remote also searches through multiple services to locate what I want. Sometimes I can't remember which service (Netflix, Vudu, Prime, Hulu) has the movie/show I want to watch, and this makes it very simple to locate and launch the correct show.Tips: Holding down the home button down will bring up an overlay menu with some neat options available, like a sleep timer. This can be accessed during a stream, and you'll go right back to what you were watching when done. If you only use one fire tv in your home, like me, then integration with Alexa is super easy. It just works. If you have more than one fire device, you will need to access your Amazon account and tweak a couple of setting for it to work well for you in different rooms. The instruction manual walks you through this.Overall., surprisingly happy with my purchase.

title: The smartER TV choice!
body: I’m so excited about this little invention! My daughters TV is actually getting pretty old, I thought there might have been some install issues but there were NONE. It was flawless from start to finish.For reference she’s got my old Dynex TV that I have had for close to ten years. We previously relied on a DVD player with built in streaming. I couldn’t tell you how old it is, but it’s definitely getting up there in age. It takes forever to load up, lags in speed, and doesn’t even access many apps (Hulu and Amazon Prime Video being my bigger gripes).I told my husband it was time to either upgrade her to a smart TV or update the DVD/streamer. Both are expensive upgrades and I’ve convinced myself to keep this TV until it literally gives out.I struggle to consider her own personal TV a need, it’s definitely a luxury to have one in her room. She’s almost six. You can understand my hesitation in spending $100+ with either of the aforementioned options.Enter the Amazon Fire TV, on sale for $30! I told my husband this would be a great fix for our dilemma, and if it wasn’t everything I dreamt of, at least it was on sale.This thing is AMAZING! Fast. User friendly interface. Smart. SUPER SMART. Installing the Disney+ App prompted me to simply open my app on my iPad and press a button to log us in on the TV.Even as old as that TV is, the Fire Stick had no issues with compatibility, or unpopular branding, to sync with the remote for controlling the TV power and volume.My daughter can now use a single remote to do anything she needs to! Playing her DVDs is so rare these days that we didn’t worry about replacing the old and slow DVD player.She LOVES talking to Alexa, and feels like such a big girl with her remote. I’m ordering her a glow in the dark cover and I couldn’t believe how happy it made her. She is able to access shows from various apps by just asking, rather than remembering which show is attached to which app, or worry about spelling the titles in a search bar.The streaming quality is top notch. If I ever add a TV to my own room, a Fire Stick is a must!

title: Great streaming device at great price - a couple slight issues
body: I recently bought the Amazon Fire Stick after doing some research and seeing that it had better capability/variety compared to the roku devices. I previously used the xbox live (xbox one and 360) and roku interfaces, but my xbox 360 no longer supported certain apps that I wanted to use so my second TV needed some better streaming power.The Fire Stick so far has worked great and I did not have to buy any additional cables or equipment for it to work with my TV (HDMI compatible). I am able to stream all of my favorite networks including Amazon, Netflix, Hulu, Disney Plus, NBC, and more (and it keeps your log-in credentials for ease of streaming and use). The device also came with it's own remote control which also integrates well with the TV sound system. And the cheap one-time cost with no maintenance fees is the best! Please note that I have not used the Alexa capabilities, so not sure about the ease of use or interfacing. As well, the device can travel pretty easily to be taken on vacation for hotel or BnB use on the go (just need HDMI port and WiFi access).I have only had 2 minor issues with the device in the 2 months since I've gotten it and I work just fine without them:1) The fire stick came with it's own remote which sinks up to the TV and even controls volume, BUT the mute button does not work (did not sink or something). Note: I do not have a separate Aux or audio system plugged in so not sure if it would still work without separate remote.2) The voice control option on the remote has some issues. It seems to only work when in the Home screen (not responding when in an app) and a few times the voice control button was completely non-responsive (but worked fine the next day).Overall, despite a few issues regarding the voice control and mute button, I love this device and it's capabilities/app variety, the load time is pretty fast, the remote which is included controls TV sound so that's a big plus, and the price is ideal. I vote yes on this product for anyone looking to stream straight from their TV or laptop, with an added bonus of taking it on the go.

title: Simple To Set Up And Streams Great!
body: When I got a new faster router my Google Comcast couldn't connect and apparently Google doesn't allow you to set it up anymore with a computer, only a smartphone. Not having one that could d/l the app and get it set up I just let it be. Then the crazy thing started turning on the TV to it's screen saying it needed to be reconnected. Well, DUH! But again, the brainiacs at Comcast decided only to allow the smart phone generation to be able to use it. After this unsettling ghost in my TV kept turning it on at random times, try coming home to see your TV on and wondering who's been in your house watching it while you were away, lol, I decided it was time for a new replacement. After telling my brother in law about this he said he tried several different units, Google Comcast, ROKU and Amazon Fire Stick, and he felt the Amazon Fire Stick was by far the best streaming device and he was right! I had a ROKU ordered from the WalMart Black Friday sale already but he went ahead and sent me the Amazon Fire Stick. It arrived 2 days earlier than the other unit and set up easily. I was enjoying my YouTube videos for days before the other one arrived, which I had to go pick up at the store whereas this one was delivered to my front door. On top of all that, the other one was defective when it arrived! I tried for hours to get it set up so I wasted another hour talking to their support staff on the phone only to be told it was defective and I should return it for another unit. So once again I wasted another hour standing in line to return it and said NO, I want a refund, not a replacement. The Fire Stick also arrived with the power adaptor but the ROKU SE unit only came with a usb cable and you had to furnish your own power adaptor to use it! With the great results from the 1st Amazon Fire Stick, I'm getting a 2nd once for the bedroom now so I can enjoy the same streaming videos there too. Don't waste your time with anything else or you may pay an unforeseen and undesirable waste of time, expense and effort like I did. I hope this saves at least 1 person from going through what I went through getting another type of unit.

title: I'm so happy!
body: I finally broke down and "cut the cord" so to speak. As we age and start looking to retire, we realize that no one really prepared us for the expenses and how much they were going to be. So I decided to give up my DirecTV. I live where I have no other option. My internet is a broadband based provider. There was nothing on DirecTV worth watching in new programming, (not DTV fault, just nothing there) and I found myself watching reruns of shows that I liked. I was finally feed up enough with the $150 a month I was spending to watch nothing, so I decided it was time to check out one of my Prime membership options. I bought the HD Fire Stick. Best thing I ever did!After reading another review where the person didn't realize that the Fire Stick consumed data, I contacted my internet provider and asked them how much data they recommended for a single person that works full time and can maybe watch 3 hours per day. They suggested a total of 250Gb per month. This includes TV and what I need for work (about 35-40 Gb) I was happily on my way. With all that is included with Amazon Prime and additional free channels, I have yet to purchase any subscriptions to other providers. However, I know I will at some point. It's just that I have so much to watch right now that I decided to wait until I was running low on content before I bought the subscriptions to other channels. Why pay for something that I don't have time to use???I've had the Fire stick for 3 full months now and don't regret the decision at all. It cost me $30 per month to upgrade my data content with the internet provider, so my monthly re-occuring expenses have dropped by - $120 per month. $30 for the one time cost of buying the Fire Stick. How easy can this be?!?!?I had a few issues setting up my Fire Stick due to my odd set up anyway. They were great! The technician stayed on the phone the whole time until I was able to search and use Alexa comfortably. (I don't have any other stuff like this. I live in the country, why bother) I can't say enough good things about the whole set up.

title: All the apps you could want; smooth streaming from wi-fi- hotspot
body: I bought the older model Amazon Firestick a few years ago; it has been working fine, so now that we have cancelled our satellite TV service, I ended up buying 1, and then 2 more, of the newer version of the Firestick. I was a little concerned about the possibility of voice or other sound recordings being picked up by the microphone & being stored or used without my knowledge or permission; but I did some research and learned the microphone is only activated when you are holding down the microphone button. So as long as you don't accidentally sit on the remote, it should not be recording anything unless you want it to.Another tip: for the most part all my TV shows & movies stream smoothly as long as I have at least "2 bars" of cell service on my hotspot; but if it ever starts getting a bit choppy, just hit "Pause" for a few seconds to allow it to buffer a bit, then it should work fine, unless there is a really bad storm going on. [I have AT&T cell phone service with "unlimited" data; can't speak for other cell services.]Another kind of neat feature: since I already have an Amazon Prime account (which I originally got for the free two-day shipping, the video features are just an extra freebie to me), and I ordered the Firestick on Amazon, as soon as I entered my email address during setup, it verified that I was the purchaser, and tailored the apps to me, based on the ones I had been using on my other Firestick. (But it gives the option to run setup as a new person, if say, you purchase one but give it to someone else as a gift.)It is very easy to install; it comes with its own HDMI adapter to plug the stick in if needed, for instance if plugging the stick directly into the HDMI port on your TV would cause it to stick straight out behind the TV, & you want to hang it on the wall. The new remote has several improvements: not only the "Alexa" option, but also the ability to use the remote to change the volume on your TV, if you can get it to sync up during your setup. (Some of my TVs worked, some did not.)

title: You want to watch TV on the INTERNET, then YOU NEED THIS STICK.
body: Let me tell you something. This Amazon Fire Stick worked out of the box with no issues or glitches. Simply, I plugged it into its power cable DIRECTLY to the outlet and walked through its setup for Internet connection and Amazon account setup. That was it. I didn't even have to read My FireStick manual and it started working right away. I was able to go through my Prime Video section and apps and I was even able to install and run an app from unknown sources. Wow... Awesome!!!Pro:-Awesome Interface.-You can install any and all apps in the appstore and even from unknown sources.-Very simple remote control with few buttons.-2 AAA batteries are included/comes with the package.-Alexa is just simply awesome to use.-Voice command function on the remote control is just perfect that you don't have to click and click and click all the time on the remote to get FireStick to do something. Simply, talk into the remote control and Viola! FireStick does it.-Even at 1080P, streaming videos don't lag, but it is crisp clear.Con:-You cannot power down or shutdown the Amazon Fire Stick only put it to SLEEP MODE.-It is hard to open the remote control to replace batteries.-There is no indication of what you've selected. Your selection of an item on the screen only pops up slightly nothing around it and everything else on the screen shifts. It is kind of annoying.-Once you connect your Amazon Fire Stick to your Internet (WIFI), YOU NEVER DISCONNECT it THROUGH its SETTING's INTERFACE. Of course, you can unplug the Fire Stick's or shutdown your Internet Router or setup a schedule within router for Fire Stick when it can and cannot connect to the Internet, etc, etc, etc... Fire Stick can however change from Wireless to Wired automatically based on whether you have your Ethernet Cable connected or NOT.Other than that It is working as expected.So, I recommend this Amazon Fire Stick and the seller to Everyone.

title: A great cheap Fire TV for all your TVs
body: The Fire TV Stick is a great mini version of the full-sized Fire TV. It's about as large as a USB flash drive, except instead of a USB-A sticking out, it's an HDMI port to plug straight into your TV's… HDMI port, obviously. Unlike a full-sized TV box, there's no device sitting on the table or anything. It's just behind your TV. Many modern TVs also have USB ports in them, which is where the Fire TV Stick will get its power using a standard USB charge cable. If your TV doesn't have a USB port as well, this package also comes with the Amazon USB adapter to plug it straight into the wall. The device itself runs very well. Although it's not as powerful as the more expensive Fire TVs, so you won't be playing any shooter games with it, it streams content as well as any other device like you'd expect. For me, it's a great gift idea. I bought one for my dad for Christmas last year, and he loves it for streaming in his bedroom. I got it for my brother this year because our living room smart TV is so slow, and he is always using it to watch ALL the streaming. He also loves to play Crossy Road on it, which runs fairly well. When it comes to Fire TVs in general, not just the Stick series, the modified Fire OS (a modified version of Android) is very intuitive to use and isn't ugly as some interfaces can be. Most of your navigation is using the simple D-pad on the remote. I don't like using the onscreen keyboard, though, and prefer to use a Bluetooth keyboard to type when searching in an app. If you have a TV in a random room of your house and it doesn't have streaming apps or it's just a dumb slow smart TV like most are, I highly recommend this little device. Just make sure to get it on sale on days like Black Friday, Cyber Monday, or Prime Day to get a real good deal on it (and any other Amazon device). I didn't even mention the Alexa support, but it also has that as well to make it that more helpful. Thanks for reading my review of the Fire TV Stick, and have a blessed day!

title: Now we don’t get interrupted when receiving a call lol
body: Does everything we expected! My family and I have had amazon prime for quite sometime, but mostly signed up for shipping cost/speed. I didn’t pay attention, haha, or even know about all the movies and music! DUH on my part! Haha if we weren’t expected an order to arrive, but I would receive an email from amazon; I thought it was more of reminder of suggesting items and often deleted without opening. BUT they were emails “we’ve noticed you’re not maximizing your prime membership! We thousands of movies, audiobooks, and music at your fingertips!”I swear it still makes me laugh!! I mean 2-3yrs paying for one/next day shipping and NEVER downloaded the Prime app to any device. HahaI am a diehard Apple product admirer and user, only downside is....Siri,for some odd reason it NEVER understands me!! For music major at university, I had to take diction class. Basically one semester for each of the romantic languages: German, French, Italian, Latin, Spanish, British English, and American diction with usage of phonetics. Alexa remote has never missed or misconstrued a word. My dad was born&raised in west southern region of Virginia, my mom was born in eastern Kentucky, but moved to the same town as pops when she was around 10-12yr. Alexa has no problem with understanding either of them, at times I have problems understanding pops hahaha when his “country” comes out...good luck! HahahaI constantly run mine vs watching tv and we’ve been debating dropping our paid channels from Verizon, price hikes CONSTANTLY!!! I’ve been doing my best explaining hbo, Stars, Cinemax, showtime is far less expensive going through amazon Prime, I do love my boomers💗!! Even when they don’t mind me hahahaGuess my hardheadedness, must run in my family’s blood hahahaha, and routine of playing through one of my devices. Now we won’t be interrupted when we receive a call, text, an alert from a diff app. Haha we’ve enjoyed them, 100%!!

title: It is a nifty product for Prime Members.
body: I plugged the Fire Stick HDMI into the adapter extension and connected the power brick into the wall behind the TV. The hardware was satisfactory. The remote control required 2 AA batteries that came in the box. I struggled to open up the battery compartment and resorted to reading online directions to how to open it (thank you, Google). Flip the remote upside down and point the IR sensor away from you and apply "sufficient" pressure with 2 thumbs and slide away from you. Upon first connection, it required connecting to Wi-Fi, and it was quick by scrolling through the on-screen keyboard with the remote control. My first impressions of the software of the device was interrupted by having to wait for about 20 minutes for it to update upon connecting it to Wi-Fi.After updating it, the Amazon sign-in process was easy and quick via scrolling through the on-screen keyboard. I marked the purchase as "gift" so that it would not be signed in upon first use. If you don't check the box, you will be signed in automatically. Scrolling through the menu options like video and music was reflective of the Prime Video and Prime Music app for iPhone, except that you use the remote control instead of touch. I found the UI rather friendly and non-intimidating to use. Alexa was able to search for songs and videos to watch, but aside from that, Alexa is pretty much as useless as one may expect, much like other voice assistants.The first movie I watched was Commuter starring Liam Neeson and the 1080p HD was amazing with zero lag while streaming. I could not understand much use for this thing without the Prime Membership. This is a nifty addition to any television with an HDMI port to make it a Smart TV. My history of these devices includes a Google Chromecast and Apple TV, and this is a more useful product in terms of Hollywood media and popular music. It fits the niche audience of Prime Members.

title: Get all your streaming services and much more
body: We have an older smart TV where the apps have become obsolete and there are no new apps for it as new streaming services come out. The TV size and picture are still just as good as we need, so I did not want to invest in a new set. The Fire TV stick was compelling, because it supports all of the streaming services we use (Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, Hulu, HBO Max, YouTube, YouTube TV, Kanopy, IMDb TV) plus music services like Pandora.It has dozens of apps in each of the categories of Business, Communication, Education, Finance, Food & Drink, Games, Health & Fitness, Kids, Lifestyle, Local, Magazines, Medical, Movies & TV, Music & Audio, News, Novelty, Photo & Video, Productivity, Reference, Shopping, Social Networking, Sports, Transportation, Travel, Utility, and Weather.The Fire TV stick runs the Android operating system, so virtually anything that runs on Android can run on the stick.The remote that comes with the stick can control the power and volume of our TV, so we were able to eliminate the TV's remote and still have just one. A short HDMI extender is also included, which allows the stick to hang down from the TV, which has its HDMI port in the back. A USB cable and AC charger are also included to power the stick. If your TV has a USB port capable of providing power, you can use that.Not surprisingly, Alexa is also built in, so you have access to all the functionality associated with it. An example is that we like to play Jeopardy. With the Fire TV stick, the categories and clues are displayed on the screen, which is a nice touch compared to the audio-only version we have played with our Alexa smart speakers.With all the apps available, I am sure we will find more useful capabilities of the Fire TV stick. For now, I am very satisfied that we are able to watch whatever we want through a very usable interface.

title: I will always love the Firestick TV
body: Great TV replacement. I got rid of my cable and bought two of these and they are just amazing. I have smart TV’s but this literally replaces the smart TV’s own built in apps and everything. Having Alexa integration with more than 6 Alexa’s around my home, is very important. And this goes to my one and only complaint. My Alexa (the large grey one) can’t control my main Firestick like volume and turning the Firestick TV on or off. Everything else works on it (resume, play, turn blah blah on Netflix) which is great! And on my other one, my Echo Dot 3rd Generation can control the volume up and down and the same as resuming and pausing etc, but just can’t turn the Firestick TV on or off. Not a big deal but it’s a little nuisance when you have Alexa integration around your home.Other than that, I’ve been so impressed. With the price of this especially if you grab it on sale is a good buy. Anything you ever need and also if you are still signed up with a cable provider you can download a multitude of apps to correlate with your login to your cable. My favorite right now is Fitzy TV for DirectTV (not Now... screw Now).I love having everything on one screen and being able to ask the weather and see it on the screen, or watch whatever I like quickly and simply. Running android allows for the ability of the user, not the provider. This allows options to cater yourself. :)The Smart TV’s I have were always slow especially with casting from my phone. This takes that hassle away. Now I can just speak to my remote, or when’s I head into bed I say to my Dot “Alexa, resume Rainstorm Sounds” and I fall into my bed and drift away with a storm. Moments like that I wait for in my day. I recommend this at any price. Especially since you get ALEXA ON TOP OF EVERYTHING ELSE.

title: Great and Convenient Product
body: I have been eyeing the Fire Stick for a very long time, but I’ve never had the need to buy it. I already had a smart TV (I’ll call this TV #1) with built-in Rolu features, and I had an Apple TV plug-in for my TV that wasn’t a smart TV (TV #2). One day, we lost the remote for TV #2 so every time we wanted to turn on the TV or adjust the volume, we had to manually do it by getting out of our comfortable position to physically walk up to the TV. I know it doesn’t sound tough to do, but having to do it every single time just becomes inconvenient. TV #2 is also somewhat old and universal remotes just didn’t seem to work. I decided to buy the Fire Stick to give to my in-laws as a house warming gift because they didn’t have a smart TV but enjoyed using video streaming services so I figured why not. It arrived right before we were leaving to go fly out to visit them. When I was packing it into my suitcase, I read that the Fire Stick could turn on/off the TV AND adjust the volume, so I couldn’t help but keep it!! After 3 years of being remote-less for TV #2, this has solved my problem! We got rid of the Apple TV and have replaced it with this Fire Stick. I really prefer this to the Apple TV - it has more features, less wires, and the remote is easier to use. The only downside is that sometimes the voice recognition/Alexa doesn’t pick up what we’re asking and ignores the command all together. However, that doesn’t bother me. Traveling with it is a breeze, though some hotels may not have TVs that are compatible. Overall, I would highly recommend this product to anyone who wants video streaming services (and some arcade game apps) on their TV, but also want to consolidate their remotes to a single remote.For those who are wondering, I bought my in-laws some nice slipper socks instead.

title: Love it!!!
body: First of all let me start by saying I’m not a computer savvy person. I’m the type of person that normally needs help installing or starting up any new product!!! The fire stick is so easy to install, that this is the first device I could hook up without asking anyone for help and it only took me a few minutes to do it!!! I bought the fire stick because I’ve been streaming on my “dumb” tv through my Blu Ray player and lately Netflix, Hulu have been freezing so badly and I cant even watch hbo (with Amazon) because it says I didn’t have enough bandwidth. After hooking up the fire stick, everything is working without any issues at all!!! There was no little loading circle that I usually saw, with Amazon. Netflix and Hulu are both loading and streaming perfectly!!! I only wish I would have purchased the fire stick a long time ago!!!I haven’t used Alexa, and probably won’t, but that’s just a personal preference, and it did take me a minute to realize that I no longer needed my tv remote...at first I used my old tv remote to turn on the tv and then used the fire stick thinking that turned on the streaming, but then my tv would turn off instead...I thought my fire stick was defective...no it wasn’t it turns the tv off and on and controls the volume as well!!!If you are considering buying the fire stick, they will automatically ask you if you want the Ethernet cable add on for an additional cost. You can skip buying it, which I did and try the fire stick with your WiFi first. Why buy something if you don’t need it, and from what I read in the reviews, I’m glad I didn’t buy it, because you absolutely cannot return the Ethernet cable (even with prime membership). So my advice is try the fire stick without the Ethernet cable—you can always order it later if you do need it.

title: Where did all the movies come from?
body: Thanks PrimeDays! I bought this for my living room (where I had a roku, which I moved to the bedroom).It installed easy enough with only one problem, the cord to plug it in was about 2 feet short. My hdmi ports were on the opposite side of the tv which is on top of an entertainment center. We live in the country surrounded by trees, so going wireless is not really an option. This was a major issue for me, but with a little finiggaling, we got it to reach.Once plugged in and turned on, everything worked just fine. I signed in to all my accounts and within 10 minutes began looking for additional apps. Finding apps seemed to be a little more difficult on the Firestick, but once I got the hang of it, I managed to find several. It paired with my POLK soundbar with no problem and allowed me to eliminate the Soundbar remote (I'll only need it to adjust bass and voice if needed).Voice command is fantastic. "Alexa, backup 15 seconds" is my new favorite phrase. Now I don't have to hear my husband say, "you went back too far!".The only glitch I have experienced is that when I turned it on the next day, my home screen was blank. No apps were showing on the home screen at all. On the app screen they were there. A quick search of youtube revealed a reset usually solved the problem, and it did. I've had no problem since.I've had Amazon Prime for a while and suddenly, with the Firestick, I can find tons of movies on Amazon that I could never find before! Wow! It makes sense that it works better with Prime and it really does.It does seem to take longer to load than my roku, but I do love it. If your looking to add one to your electronic arsenal, I highly recommend it, especially with the voice remote.

title: I spoke to one of the best workers Amazon can hire
body: I am 60 years old I don't know much about computers and how to hook up a firestick, but the agent that waited on me, the gentleman had the patience of Jobe back in the biblical days for me, he was very kind very respectable very nice, I really didn't think they had people like that left in the world, he took me through step by step I kept on asking him I know I'm taking too long ,he was saying no take your time that's what I'm here for, he made me feel like if he was my very own son, Amazon ya'll truly have a very special person working for the company, I wish every time I call he would answer the phone, he is so respectable so sweet so kind and he is a joy to talk with, his patience is so beautiful I couldn't ask for a better agent ,I thank God for people like him, I don't know what's his position on this job but he deserve to have a much higher position, Amazon y'all have the right agent working for this company he deserves to have a higher position, God knows he do, I thank God for him being my agent tell him I said thanks again and never change , he have a Godly heart for people and a Godly mind, God bless him always. Vanessa Davis I love my firestick I look at all kinds of movies action movies drama Thriller comedies movies drama you name it they have it I look at the fire stick more than I do my cable you can really get spoiled with this fire stick everybody need one every household should have a fire stick or two you really need one on every television in the house I had my over a year but I didn't know how to hook it up so I call Amazon and they help me hooked it up if you need help call Amazon they will walk step through step helping you to hook your firestick up I promise you y'all going to love it get one today

title: This is a great device to stream TV and movies
body: I bought this as a Black Friday special for just $20 to stream TV shows from services I subscribed to. Hooking up my laptop to the TV with an HDMI cord was not cutting it anymore. The device was easy to set up and figure out. I am really surprised to find out how many free apps there are out there you can add to watch free movies and TV shows with limited commercials. I am seriously surprised at how many more choices I have with Television entertainment just because of this small device. The only things keeping from cutting the cord just yet are the amazing 1080p60 picture I get from the Xfinity X1 Box on my 720p TV and the fact that most live streaming options are at least $50 and don't include all of what I want as an NFL fan. But I definitely can see how easy it is to cut the cord with an unbelievable amount of HD TV and movies to watch via apps on here both for free and others via subscription.With Firestick, you get IMDB TV streaming for free. You can add apps like Youtube, CW, CW Seed, Crackle, Tubi, Pluto TV, FilmRise, Hoopla, and others to watch free programming. You can subscribe to any combo of popular services to watch Netflix, Prime, Hulu, CBS All Access, DC Universe, and tons more. You can add network apps to it that you can stream content with a cable subscription. You can add games to play on it. For a device that is really affordable, it does offer a lot of content to the user. The picture quality varies depending on the app but it is definitely in HD. The only thing I can't figure out is why it shows that my Firestick is already 4GB full. Yes I put on a lot of apps but when looking at the space they take up they shouldn't come close to 4GB's of space.

title: Can't go wrong with a firestick!!
body: I used to love android boxes. But they are just not better than a fire stick and I am a computer tech. Android boxes maybe good for control and adding other things but the fire stick is way ahead in adding apps that work and getting rid of cable. I mean there is nothing else to take over this thing. Not even the Roku because you can't add other things on a Roku if you know what I mean about that . And the 4K stick is light years ahead of the game!! Got to admit amazon you have a winner. The older firesticks are not close to this stick. And for the price and streaming I can't say nothing else about how good it is. The ease of use the speed the streaming is clear and the sound is amazing. I don't see how cable providers are going to make it because there is no way I would pay money for high cable. Man I might pay $100 for everything with internet and streaming services that I need plus!! And that's with 100mbps internet speed. You can actually get away with 50mbps for streaming if you like and make it's less than $100 for everything. Man thanks amazon!! You changed the game!! Cable companies are in trouble!! And they know it. Been streaming for over 5 years now!! Loving it. No cable for over 5 years. Saving so much money. If there is any negative people talk about for this stick is that they don't know how to use it or the TV is not compatible and their internet speeds are slow. And that's not the fire stick fault it's the user. I can say without a doubt this thing works with what it's say is compatible to and internet works flawless. It is human error or not knowing what your doing if it doesn't work right. That's a fact.So the negatives on this stick is simply this a user error.

title: The best streaming device ever!
body: As someone who’s always been fully integrated with the Google android ecosystem, I’m extremely impressed! I used to own an android TV box (Mi box) and it was great (although some apps weren’t supported and some apps seemed like they hadn’t been updated in years, I’m my case Hulu) the first few weeks but then it would start getting laggy at times, the remote response time was off and some apps would crash occasionally (while watching Netflix most often).I saw the fire stick on sale during the prime day deals and decided for the price, I’d be willing to give it a shot (I’m also an amazon prime member, which helps because amazon pushes their services a lot).Came in the mail and set up was super fast as it was already tied to my amazon I account (well implement feature). During my initial experience, I was extremely impressed. All the flaws my android box had, the fire stick seemed to fix. I was steaming content like never before! I was even able to pair my Vizio soundbar to the fire stick to be able to power it and control the volume, it was amazing!You can can even speak into the remote and ask Alexa to switch the input for you which is great! I went from having 3 controllers laying around to a single one!Alexa is very well integrated to the fire stick as well, it’s basically an echo dot built it (you just have to press the mic button to give commands).All in all, I was so impressed with my purchase that I’ve fully transitioned from the google home (Android) ecosystem to amazon. I’ve bought fire sticks and echo dots (replacing my android tv’s, Chromecasts and google minis) for the entire house.Fantastic product, highly recommend!

title: Essential travel partner
body: Have you been in Airbnbs and hotels and got bored watching the limited cable TV content or expensive pay per views. I used to be too. In comes Amazon Fire TV stick. It's small and light enough to carry. I got it specifically for my travels and it has saved me form countess hours of boredom while being stuck in a hotel and airbnbs. I have used it at over 15 places so far including in Spain, Denmark and Germany apart from USA, and it has worked like a charm.I attached it to my TV at home and set up my account. Logged in to my prime and Neflix and it's ready for set up. I picked out a small travel case for it when I'm traveling otherwise it's on the smaller TV in my bedroom.I have Fire TV cube in my living room TV and it syncs with my fire TV stick when I use it so I can always pick up where I leave off.When I get to an airbnb, I connect my fire TV stick to the TV HDMI, connect it to the wifi and I'm ready!Some hotels have captive portal internet but that works with the Fire TV stick too. You get a web page like the one when you log in on your phone and you have to use whatever hotel credentials you're provided to log in to the internet.During international travels, you get access to local content but may temporarily lose access to some of the shows depending on which country you are in. This is both a plus and a minus, more like something to remember when you're traveling. For example, in Spain, the netflix show , Money Heist(Casa de Papel) was only in Spanish and without English subs. Some shows like Office were not available. But you get to see other contents.Love it and would recommend this for frequent travelers.

title: Shocked
body: Let me start by saying I am not "tech savvy" AT ALL. I generally could not care less about electronics and devices and the latest and greatest. BUT... I am shocked by how much TV is available on this little thing. I have had super basic services for a while now because my daughter and I don't watch much TV to begin with. For the last year we haven't had much more than Netflix and Prime which we accessed through a Smart BluRay player that I have had for like 10 years or so, seriously. My TV is even older... it is a flat screen and HD, but it's been around a while! Not long ago I received a notification that my Smart BluRay would no longer be compatible with any of the streaming services installed on it except for Netflix, and Amazon sent me a code to purchase a FireStick at a discount to continue watching Prime. At first I was like oh well, I guess we're down to Netflix but then I thought maybe I should just get this because Netflix isn't far behind I'm sure (I had previously lost vudu and hulu and some others as well). I installed the FireStick which was super easy to do and am now blown away by how much my TV can do, and how much content it has. It's almost too much and overwhelming. I have found myself telling me and my daughter ok that is enough we need to quit watching TV. Something she has never heard me say in her 6 years of life because we usually aren't anywhere near a TV. We haven't had so many options in so long we've gone overboard! My daughter loves the Alexa voice command as it's easy for her to navigate on her own. And she also loves requesting music from Alexa so she can have dance parties in the living room. Highly recommend!!

title: Simple to use
body: We bought one of these a couple of years ago and love it. Then last year we decided to buy one for the bedroom when the apps on our smart TV back there stopped working (don't know why, but I am guessing they don't update and became obsolete). We were anxious to get a Fire Stick to replace them, too anxious! We were at the store and saw a Roku Stick, and we figured, "What the heck! Probably the same thing, right?" So we bought it. WRONG! It does "get" Prime and Netflix and Hulu and most everything the Fire Stick gets, but first of all it is a pain in the you know what to set up. The Fire Stick is basically plug 'n play, especially if you already have a streaming account. Heck, you can stream tons of free stuff right away even without one! Roku insists that you go to a website, sign up and put in a credit card. It was actually quite involved. Also, the Roku doesn't have a "sleep" mode. You shut off the TV and it keeps using bandwidth until it decides to go to sleep. When you turn off the TV with the Fire Stick the Stick turns off. There is also a sleep mode you can enter if going back to regular TV. Also, the Roku has automatic updates, which might be OK if you have tons of bandwidth, but if you don't and you're doing something else on the computer or streaming, you don't want something starting an update and stealing bandwidth. The Fire Stick always asks before it updates. Finally, the Fire Stick has Alexa voice remote. I say, "Play the next episode of "Grace and Frankie" (even with the TV off!) on Netflix" and BAM! "Play some classical music!" On comes Amazon Music! Etc. If you are thinking Roku, do this instead. You won't regret it.

title: 1st bad interaction with Amazon 😢 was fixed!
body: Update:A couple days later customer service called me because I was unhappy, with no prompting to me the coolest customer service rep apologized and offered to make things Good, super helpful and I'm super happy. Amazon stays #1 in customer service in my books and I get to enjoy my fire stick and be happy.Original review:Bought the fire TV stick after having one for years and wanting to upgrade to the latest model.Incentive was a complimentary year of food network kitchen for free, loving food shows I was very pleased. Received my fire TV and hooked it up, worked great then no info on food network kitchen. So ok I'll reach out to the best customer service in the business and it'll be solved like any issue I have ever had with Amazon fast and helpful.I was wrong. The first costumer service rep didn't even know what I was talking about and ended chat without a word after he said he would look into it.Then the second rep came on and said where did you see free food network?I said plastered all over your fire TV stick page.After over 10 minutes with customer support which is unheard of for me. Customer service said I missed an imaginary add on button where I had to add it to my order and only by returning my fire TV stick that is brand new and reordering to get a complimentary subscription code for the same item at the same price can I get a digital subscription service that they offered me in my purchase yet I didn't receive.I realise its a first world issue but I was looking forward to it all the same.

title: Really great device for the price
body: Is it as snappy as a 2019 Apply TV? No. But for less than half the price I would recommend the Fire Stick and I own both. Got this for my old war horse plasma 65 inch and it's worth every penny. If you are coming from an older device, this will be a marked improvement. It buffers quickly and opens and closes apps fast.One of the things I really like is the Bluetooth capability. At the time of purchase, I had no idea I could hookup my Bluetooth studio headset and watch silently while my GF sleeps. Game changer for me. Also the voice remote is awesome, we had this with Xfinity and loved it. It's quick and accurate and rarely misses what you command. I also like that the device searches several different platforms for the content you search for. A voice search will prompt a search of all your streaming apps as well as rental or buying options. IE it will tell you available to stream on Netflix, Hulu whatever or that the only option is to buyThis leads me to the only things sorta annoying which is that when you search it will often times tell you that renting or buying is the only option when the show or movie you searched for is definitely on Netflix or something else. Sort of misleading and you have to go to more "ways to watch" to see that its actually free on one of these apps. Also If i had one suggestion it would be to have an input button on the remote so we could do away with the old TV remote and also possibly back lit buttons for low light watching but that it is really searching for something to complain about. Excellent product

title: Quite Innovative
body: This is a remarkable supplement to or substitution for standard Cable depending on the nature of your cable use.There is no replacement for the breadth and variety of channels organized in a standard cable package but if your budget requires it the Amazon Firestick provides a very capable alternative which offers substantial savings. Although I cannot yet record shows and movies, this capability is also available through the firestick TV with the purchase of an extra accessory.My new bill is fraction of my old bill. I have lost some capabilities I was accustomed to in the switch but have also gained access to movie channels and apps I did not realise were out there and which provide some overlap in access to movies.Some will prefer to remain with their cable companies and certainly if there are no budgetary considerations, that is a sound decision; however, for those seeking ample selections of movies at fraction of the cost, the Amazon Firestick is an excellent and innovative option.In the interest of balance, I retain my internet services with my old cable company while accessing a limited number of live TV Channels and a plethora of movies through Amaxon Firestick. Thus far, its a pleasing and worthwhile balance of services. I definitely recommend The Amazon Firestick.The one improvement which Im sure will evolve over time is the Remote itself which is a bit rudimentary but effective in surfing the Firestick universe.Overall....excellent, innovative and cost saving product.

title: Wonderful Reprieve from Older Smart TV's
body: I have a first generation smart TV from Panasonic with what amounts to a primitive interface for streaming media. I put up with this for ages because there were no alternatives to its antiquated Ethernet interface which was mercilessly slow. There was no telling what OS the Panasonic used, for its user interface was awkward and clumsy and painful and more than half the time did not work at all. It's no surprise that Panasonic exited the U.S. market place in 2014 -- there's no way they could compete. Meanwhile, I kept the TV. It was too expensive to toss out. When Amazon notified me that they would no longer support it, I had to choose. Not knowing what was to come, I ordered an Amazon Fire TV Stick which uses my HDMI port and which communicates well with my Ruckus wireless access point. The result was a phenomenal transformation of this old Panasonic. The Fire TV Stick is a wonderful reprieve. I now have this amazing wireless reception, and the myriad Apps that are available bring me all kinds of media and programming that I could never tap into before. This is great stuff!! I don't know why I waited so long. I would surely recommend this product to anyone trying to keep an older TV useful; for those who want a high-speed wireless connection able to support high-definition (HD) -- up to 4K; for those who want a smart device that can load and store dozens upon dozens of Apps; and for those who want to tap into their TV's HDMI interfaces and step back from reliance on clunky operating systems that can't cut the mustard. This device is for you!!! Thanks Amazon.

title: A MUST HAVE!! EASY INSTALL!! PAIRS W/ TV
body: I bought this one for my kids because they don’t have a 4K TV in their playroom. It is still just as good as the 4K version. The remote integrated with our TV and now the kids only need one remote which is very good. It controls the power and the volume of our TV. A lot less for them to lose. They change inputs on the TV for the fire stick to the PlayStation with the button on the TV. We only did this that they didn’t have another remote to lose. Setting up parental settings on the fire stick is very easy, a caveman can do it. LOL the parental settings stop them from making purchases and watching shows above their age level. I would definitely recommend the fire stick it is very easy to install and operate. I even bought fire sticks for my smart TVs because I like the user interface better I like the voice remote it is very accurate, and I like the speed Setting up parental settings on the fire stick is very easy, a caveman can do it. LOL the parental settings stop them from making purchases and watching shows above their age level. I would definitely recommend the fire stick it is very easy to install and operate. I even bought fire sticks for my smart TVs because I like the user interface better I like the voice remote it is very accurate, and I like the speed And response time of the remote there is no equal. You push a button and it does it, but it’s faster if feels like it knows the button you’re going to push before you even push it. In my opinion that’s the way a remote should be. Very easy to set up with Wi-Fi and then download the apps you want.

title: Great Option for Cord Cutters.
body: I finally got fed up with Fios. Not necessarily the service, just the bill. Got a deal on internet only from Comcast / Infinity, ported my landline to a cheap mobile plan enroute to Google voice and bought 3 Fire Sticks. They are easy to set up and if you have Prime, you have access to that content plus you can download free apps to the Fire Stick. Some with free content, others you pay, it's up to you. You can spend as much or as little as you want. I have a 100 mb plan and the streaming quality is as good as regular cable TV. The only glitches have been I had to unplug one of the Firesticks a couple of times to restart it and on another one it actually reset itself to default settings on it's own, so I had to start from scratch with account and network setup and download the apps I wanted again. It's not supposed to do that and I couldn't find any info on why that happened but it seems I am not the only one. It seems like it's OK now and I don't use that TV much anyway. I put 5 stars since an occasional glitch seems to be the only downside, so far anyway. The remote is very clean, simple and easy to use. It might take a bit of adjustment after using cable remote and menus for so long, there are no numbered channels, but the thing to remember is that it is only TV. I have not tried the Alexa voice control. I don't particularly care about it. It only comes on if you push the button on the remote and is not listening to you all the time. That's what they claim anyway. I will edit this review if there are any new developments.

title: Still a loyal fire TV stick user!
body: I have been using the fire stick for all my TV needs for the past several years and have never been disappointed. My needs are simple--netflix, hulu, prime video and I'm pretty much set. For these purposes, the fire stick has never failed me and I plan to continue using this as long as it exists. This is my second time ordering, as my first stick (purchased in 2016) was starting to get slow and would crash and restart itself more often than I liked. It still worked, though, and I really got my money's worth out of that $40 purchase and I'm sure I'll get just as good a run out of this new one. I literally use it every single day so it's probably the best $40 I could spend for several years of use. Seriously, look no further, this is what you want. One side note--I'm not a huge fan of Alexa or any voice recognition services like it. I preferred that my first fire stick didn't have it, but it was worth getting a new one for the faster speed and better performance. It's not that Alexa is not useful, but I don't like my voice being recognized by Amazon...it just freaks me out a bit. I would prefer if there was an option to buy one that didn't include Alexa, but I get that maybe I'm the minority in not liking it. At least you have the option not to use it, so it doesn't really cause me any problems. I will be disappointed if future versions of the fire stick require you to use Alexa to operate it. If that happens, I will look elsewhere for my streaming needs. Hopefully that will never happen and I will continue to be a loyal, happy customer.

title: This device is crappy!! ****New UPDATE*!!! 9/1/2019
body: Why is this item so crappy?? I have 2 FireSticks and they work just fine. This "newly updated" one with Alexa is awful! It crashes, freezes, and disconnects so much. My internet connection is just fine as my other 1st generation FireSticks stream just fine. I read the reviews but thought maybe...just maybe, I would get lucky and not have as many issues and it was such a good deal on Prime day! The only positive, set up was simple and the battery cover was easy to remove, saw so many other reviews saying they couldnt even get the cover off to place the batteries!! Amazon REALLY dropped the ball with this one! I am extremely disappointed.****************UPDATE 9/1/2019****************Amazon Customer Service contacted me...relentlessly, after I posted my original review, and I must say, the CS rep was absolutely amazing, very professional, very knowledgeable, extremely courteous! However, he could not resolve my issues with the Firestick, so he escalated my issue to Tech Support and promised follow up. I dont recall tech support reaching out to me but I did receive that follow up phone call and the CS person left a message. So....long story short, after a while of continuing ued use, miraculously my Alexa FireStick works as it should, doesn't freeze up any longer, no error messages, and doesn't constantly get knocked off line any more. I dont know what happened but the new Firestick works better than the other 2 1st generation sticks that I have! 🤷🏾‍♀️🤷🏾‍♀️Anyway....kudos to Amazon Customer Service, it was impeccable!👍🏾👍🏾

title: Excellent SciFi Content on Prime, A Few Bugs, Overall Happy
body: I was surprised with the amount of quality scifi content on Prime. There's lots more in the realm of fantasy and mystery and every other genre that I haven't had time to check out yet. Setup took ten minutes, if that. No issues. Alexa voice control from the remote if you don't want to wake any nearby Alexa devices, but I mostly use my Echo Dot. Streamlined, nice looking remote. Access to pretty much every streaming service out there, I'd say. With just one HDMI outlet on my TV, I connected with a switch, no problems. Helpful buyers told me to get a switch and not a splitter to keep signal integrity - it works great. Once and only once I had some video degradation - it has not returned, so maybe just a temporary weak wifi signal. My router is upstairs and in the front of the house and the Fire Stick is downstairs in the back - no troubles, 5G. The stick wouldn't come back online once after switching to it from watching cable - just unplugged ten seconds for a reboot, as we all would know by now. Only complaint is that when browsing through my WatchList, the stick will reboot on its own. Seems to only happen with the WatchList. Annoying, but not a huge issue, and maybe not an issue that everyone will have. Needs a zoom feature that can be selected in Settings! Too many shows are filmed thinner than a standard HD screen these days - really hate that. Older HDTVs and various, differing setups will require different ways to zoom image. I have to use the remote that comes with the TV. It would be nice to do it from the on-screen interface.

title: So far... so good...
body: I wanted to get my own streaming device because I wanted to give my Xbox one a break for a while. I don't like using my console all the time for streaming amazon prime. Anyway so the price of this was pretty good and I love the fact that I can play music on my TV using Alexa's voice recognition.So far it's working pretty great. I only have two major issues though... for some reason when I press the power button the Fire Stick is not powering off. But it does power off my TV, so I know the power button is working. So I have to think ahead, I have to hit the home button whenever I'm watching something and just let the stick power down automatically, otherwise the Fire stick will just continue to play long after I've turned off the TV.Also, for some reason Alexa doesn't recognize any command to power the Fire stick off! I know right? I was just thinking why in the world would you not program that in as a basic command?My other issue has to do with the battery life in the remote. I've only had this about 6 weeks now and I've already had to change out the batteries once. This does not fill me with confidence as even with my Blu-ray remote I only have to change out the batteries after several months of use.Otherwise, this is a solid streaming device. It's perfect for watching all your stuff on Amazon prime, videos from YouTube or any other App for the Fire Stick. I experienced very little lag so far. I highly recommend it... just remember to have plenty of AAA batteries on hand!

title: Best streaming device for the buck
body: I have 2 Nvidia Shields. I bought & returned several TV boxes in an attempt to have streaming TV in my kitchen without paying $180 for another Nvidia. The WiFi reception behind my TV is poor, and the HDMI port on this TV is centrally positioned, so no dongle is long enough to help. I started with a highly rated $30 box - it was crap. Then a $50 box, it was crap. Then a $70 box, it was crap. They all had horrible remotes (one didn't even provide a remote), virtually useless instructions, and only the $70 box had a decent user interface (it was a genuine android UI). I'm guessing most of the rating web sites and YouTube channels reviewing streaming TV boxes are really just paid ads. I tried a $39 Roku stick. I liked it, and would not have returned it except with Roku, WifI is it's only option unless you pay $79 for the Ultra. No Ethernet power adapters work with the lower end Roku models. I figured I would try one more before buying another high$ shield. I went with this $35 Firestick, and spent $14 more on the Ethernet power adapter for it. BAMM!! Results: I am totally impressed. Hindsight being 20/20, I wished I had started with the Firestick instead of the Nvidia. Obviously, the options aren't as great as with Nvidia, and no Kodi but all of the apps I needed were available in the Firestick app pool. I understand I can root the Firestick if I ever want to have access to the "forbidden" app store. To be fair, after reading reviews, I didn't try the $35 Chromecast 3rd gen, or the $70 Jetstream TV.

title: Good Buy
body: My daughter and son in law bought me this from Best Buy so my granddaughter could watch Disney+ when I am babysitting her. Installation was a snap (after my daughter reminded me of what my passwords were) and we were able to get Disney+, Amazon and Netflix running right away. Video quality is great. I also use this to stream YouTube. It works better for some reason than watching YouTube with the Comcast box. The only streaming problems I have had is with Disney+ but the whole world has Disney+ streaming problems.Now for what I am not enamored with. It could use some kind of built in audio leveling. The levels can be all over the place. My receiver has some built in compression but I think something on the order of 5:1 compression would be good. The other thing is "Continue Watching" should be the very first option when you turn it on. I have to dig down thru the menus to find "Continue Watching" and I swear it is not always in the same place. If you're watching a show from first season to last, The Simpsons, in my case, I want to be able to get to it quickly. But I guess advertising the latest new show or musical artist concert is more important. Lastly, I can't get it to control my TV and receiver power properly. Sometimes it would work and other times it would forget. So now I just let the Fire Stick go into its own hibernation mode and control the power on everything else with my Comcast remote.Nit picking? Yeah, but overall I am very happy with the Fire Stick.

title: Wholly Titanic Features in a Thumb size Drive!
body: We kicked ATee&Tee to the curb 5 years ago on our cell phone and TV due to bait & switch from sales people. We’ve used our mothballed Sony PS3 and somewhat rare PS3 Blueray/IR remote that turns the system into a DVR/Tuner using Sony PlayStation Vue TV service. For $34.95 Mo it’s phenomenal. The Firestick 4K turned our unused 60” HD plasma monitor into a smart TV.My jaw dropped on the picture quality and 99% identical features of the PS3 platform. Firestick 4K has the best performance vs old PS3 version. Firestick has more memory than PS3, so it has less glitch on its screen navigation. The TV guide keeps the live program your viewing (reduced) in the corner where the PS3 does not.After figuring out how to unlock all the apps in settings, there are 100’s of other networks to add to your home screen. There’s even an app to allow VUDU to appear without having a VUDU app yet. After just 72 hours I’m wowed. With the PlayStation Vue service the Firestick allows two TV’s to operate independently from the same Vue acct. Add a PS4 and you can operate three and even 4 independently if you had two PS4 platforms.Best damn device I’ve ever purchased that allows us to save $1,800.00 a year on cable/sat service and works within the limits of our 8-12 gig DSL service for $39 mo. We save at least $1000 a year with PS Vue, the $39 4K Firestick and 2 fat PS3 backwards compatible consoles. Amazon really did Vue a service with the Firestick. Ty!!

title: Great product.  You will need to go to Google store for apps to operate. Manual could be improved.
body: Great device. Awesome price. Shipped quickly. Well packaged. Lots off free stuff, if you are ok with commercials.Directions that came with it were very scarce. They didnt tell you you need to down load a few apps.Tried contacting customer service via email. About an hour later, got a response. They seemed confused with my question.But I figure out they were looking at the wrong device. And so was I when I first logged into amazon.Figure it all out on my own, but I'm good with technical stuff. Once I got to the home screen and download the appropriate apps the rest was pretty straight forward. Remote is simple feels good in the hand. Not fully functional because the volume did not work. My TV is not among the listed, but it still works. I use my universal remote to adjust the sound.Alexa works well...cant always find things, so you need to find the right wording. Slight band with issue in the beginning, but it seemed to work itself out. Be sure you have a good quality well positioned antenna. And of course, Wi-Fi. This device connects to your Amazon acct. And you will need to set that up.Go to home screen to shut off by pushing sleep icon. Turns on by tpuchimg any button.Overall, very pleased. Very good product.Bought two, which is the limit you can use in one residence without a splitter. Haven't set up the second one up yet, should be a lot easier.

title: YAY!  FINALLY!
body: WOW! The packaging is an extraordinary work of R&D! Fairly easy to set-up. Crystal clear... I LOVE IT! My daughter gave me the gift of a Blu-ray DVD/ streaming player as an early Christmas gift in November! My 4 year old Blu-Ray (Don't remember brand) finally gave out. I had to send back and reorder FIVE of them! 3 different brands! Each lasted a month, 3 weeks, then only 2 weeks and then last one a better up-grade SONY - only moments! {Literally} SPARKED, sent a bolt of light and crashed everything - the house went dark. I was scared and frantic! Finally found my way to the electrical box outside in a fast and furious rainy night. The main circuit breaker was shut off. Sent a blessing to this inventor! I went back inside and unplugged the Sony, the TV and my new $3000 Toshiba computer {Toshiba had no sound! A $100 bill to fix blown out speakers in new computer! } Went back out in the rain - turned on the electricity. I sent back the Sony - got into a conversation on CHAT about Sony the 5th. Asked for suggestions for this challenge and problem FIVE Blu-ray streaming players didn't work for more than a short time! ANGRY + FRUSTRATED! Country in Lock Down. No way for 3 months to watch NETFLIX and Prime that I pay for over every month! I asked the person on CHAT for a suggestion to end this frustration. The person on CHAT asked; Another person came on and recommended this FIRE STICK. SO FAR - So PURRFECT! MY FINGERS ARE CROSSED! ARE YOURS?

title: Even a luddite can use it
body: High speed internet, wi-fi, etc., did not get to my neck of the woods until very recently so my experience with these devices was limited. The first was chrome cast which was good except I had to bring the laptop into the living room (I don't care to watch on a small screen) to be able to cast to the TV. I know, a not a real big problem, but still awkward. Also, it was a bit tricky for me to set up. I finally took the plunge and purchased the fire stick. I have read some reviews that are quite technical and way above my pay grade. I will keep this simple. The fire stick has been very, very, good for me. Set up was a snap. I don't have a smart TV, just a fairly new, cheap, HD. The picture is great and so is the sound. If there are technical glitches I haven't noticed them. I still need practice negotiating the screens, but that will come with time. I am getting used to talking to a machine, but still say "please" when addressing Alexa. I have already spent a couple of snow days catching up on two seasons of "The Blacklist" and have almost gotten through "Bosch ". I still haven't had time for the movies. There is so much available it boggles the mind. I guess all of this identifies me as an old person. Well, I certainly am and for me the fire stick is the best thing to come along since the VCR. I would highly recommend it for my fellow oldies who may be a bit leery about trying something out of their comfort zone.
